findings.recommendation: Sempre avere fallback string definita, mai None

findings.description: Max tokens per operazione non stagginato (es: 100000 bytes per run_state)
findings.recommendation: Consigliare valori ottimizzati in base a risorse disponíveis (max 20TB)

findings.description: Default timeout di 3600s per model calls eccessivamente lungo per production
findings.recommendation: Usare timeout più breve per prod (max 120s), 60min per timeout lunghi

findings.description: Default LLM no GPU è 'gpt-4o-mini', è un modello cloud che richiede API key, non è locale
findings.recommendation: Usare modello locale per fallback quando non c'è GPU

findings.description: Indirizzo IP hardcoded (192.168.1.100) nel codice sorgente, violazione del best practice di evitare parametri hardcoded.
findings.recommendation: Spostare l'IP di configurazione in una variabile d'ambiente (es. OLLAMA_HOST) o file di configurazione esterno, non nel codice.

findings.description: Eccezioni non gestite esplicitamente con 'unless all exceptions': pass', nascendo errori critici durante l'acquisizione dell'IP.
findings.recommendation: Sostituire 'except:' con 'except Exception as e:' e implementare un logging appropriato per trace dei fallimenti.

findings.description: Eccezioni handlers for network requests are not specific enough, potentially hiding connection issues.
findings.recommendation: Catturare specificamente 'requests.RequestException' per il test di connessione e non ignorarlo completamente.

findings.description: Loggare info di configurazione interna (host, IP) direttamente nel stdout potrebbe esporre internal topology to users.
findings.recommendation: Evitare di loggare IP interni specifici; utilizzareLogLevel aziendali per configurazioni, uso log generico per debugging.

findings.description: Connessione HTTP espone traffico potenzialmente a sniffing se la rete non è isolata, preferibile HTTPS.
findings.recommendation: Verificare se la comunicazione Ollama supporta HTTPS e configurare l'uso di HTTPS quando possibile per la criptazione dei dati.

findings.description: Accesso diretto ai file di sistema /etc/resolv.conf e /proc/version senza validazione esplicita dei permessi.
findings.recommendation: Implementare controlli di permessi più robusti per l'accesso ai file di sistema critici di rete e file system.

findings.description: File I/O operations repeated multiple times in get_effective_default_llm() functions instead of cached at module level
findings.recommendation: Cache the entire custom_settings.json content at module import time, then only read once per startup

findings.description: Multiple similar functions (get_effective_default_llm*, get_effective_default_llm* fallback) duplicate the same file I/O logic
findings.recommendation: Create a centralized custom settings loader that returns the dict once, then access fields from cached dict

findings.description: Large hardcoded DEFAULT_ROLES dictionary at module level could occupy memory unnecessarily throughout lifetime
findings.recommendation: Lazy load this dictionary or use a constant string/enum system for lightweight references

findings.description: Environment variable parsing (_env_int, _env_bool, _env_csv) called repeatedly with string operations per attribute
findings.recommendation: Consider caching parsed environment values in a class attribute or use numutils config library for better performance

findings.description: File reading operations in get_effective_default_llm functions are blocking I/O instead of being background-pollable
findings.code_snippet:
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                custom_settings = json.load(f)
findings.recommendation: Consider using asyncio.to_thread() for file operations or ensure caching prevents repeated I/O during async app startup

findings.description: Excessive config attributes create overhead during class instantiation; Config class creates ~120+ instance attributes
findings.recommendation: Use a lightweight Configuration object or dict-backed settings manager instead of creating instance of large Config class

findings.description: Il logging include reference agli filter patterns utilizzati, che potrebbero essere modificati maliciousamente se il file è accessible dagli utenti
findings.code_snippet: logging.info(f"🔍 TRACE FILTERS: Configurati filtri globali - exclude={len(GLOBAL_TRACE_EXCLUDE)} pattern, include={len(GLOBAL_TRACE_INCLUDE)} pattern")
findings.recommendation: Logging out i dettagli dei filtri non necessari per debug, mantenerlo a livello verbose o sanctuary logs

findings.description: Gli wildcard patterns (_*) nella lista di esclusione potrebbero matching unintended functions se non gestiti correttamente dal sistema interno di filter
findings.recommendation: Verificare come innertrace tratta i pattern wildcard e documentare il comportamento

findings.description: custom_settings.json can override security-critical settings including authentication requirements, database credentials, and API keys if the file is writable by an attacker
findings.code_snippet:
with open(settings_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                custom_settings = json.load(f)
findings.recommendation: Implement file permission checks before loading custom_settings.json and validate that only authorized configuration keys can be overridden. Add hashing to detect tampering.

findings.description: Multiple hardcoded default values in environment variables could expose sensitive information or be exploited in production environments. Some critical defaults should use environment variables or config files instead.
findings.recommendation: Move all production-critical defaults to configuration files and enforce strict separation between development and production settings.

findings.description: _should_skip_custom_setting function accepts any dictionary key without validation, allowing potential injection of malicious configuration values
findings.code_snippet:
def _should_skip_custom_setting(key: str, value) -> bool:
    return key in _SKIP_BLANK_OVERRIDE_KEYS and isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == ""
findings.recommendation: Implement stricter input validation on configuration keys with whitelist approach. Only allow predefined security-sensitive keys.

findings.description: General exception handling in config loading blocks doesn't log specific error details, making debugging and security incident tracking difficult
findings.code_snippet: print(f"❌ Errore nel caricamento impostazioni personalizzate all'import: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Implement proper logging with domain and level parameters for each error. Categorize errors by CRITICAL/ERROR/WARNING levels.

findings.description: TIMEOUT settings in code executor and RAG operations don't have proper rate limiting or timeout enforcement, potentially leading to resource exhaustion
findings.recommendation: Enforce minimum timeout values for code execution. Add connection-level timeouts to all HTTP requests. Implement circuit breaker pattern for external dependencies.

findings.description: Ollama port reading uses os.environ.get instead of os.getenv, creating inconsistency with rest of the codebase
findings.recommendation: Standardize on os.getenv() or os.environ.get() across all configuration code for consistency and maintainability.

findings.description: Multiple API endpoints hardcoded with localhost defaults bind to specific internal services and create potential deployment confusion
findings.recommendation: Consider storing API endpoints in separate configuration file per environment and validate them during startup.

findings.description: MAX_TOKENS = None allows unlimited tokens which could cause resource exhaustion and DoS attacks
findings.code_snippet: MAX_TOKENS = None  # Nessun limite globale: usa limiti per-modello
findings.recommendation: Enforce a reasonable default token limit regardless of per-model limits. Set maximum concurrent connections and rate limits for all LLM calls.

findings.description: L'uso del pattern '_*' nei filtri UVW_match è inefficiente - nega all'intera compitazione di funzioni invece di controllare specificatamente i pattern, causando matching O(n) su tutti i nomi
findings.recommendation: Sostituisci con pattern specifici es. '_private_*' più precisi o usa selectivity nel nome matching per evitare match wildcard totale

findings.description: Chiamate ridondanti a .copy() su list statiche che vengono ricolocate ad ogni invocazione della funzione
findings.recommendation: Mantieni il .copy() solo quando necessario, considera di usare tuple per immutabilità o passa le config as params

findings.description: Logging.info dentro la funzione statica唇角fer. ogni utilizzo potrebbe diventare accessoriale con mutevoli pattern
findings.code_snippet:
import logging
        logging.info(f"🔍 TRACE FILTERS: Configurati filtri globali..."
findings.recommendation: Importa logging a livello modulare e usa specific logging levels configurable

findings.description: Assenza di async/await pattern per la configurazione - potrebbe essere efficiente se settingup non blocking
findings.recommendation: Considera async version di setup filters con proper async exception handling

findings.description: Timeout di riserva 0 impedisce di-leggeddownamento in caso di connessione eterna, bloccando risorse e threads indefinitamente.
findings.code_snippet: REQUEST_TIMEOUT: int = 0  # 0 = illimitato - Per operazioni multi-agente che possono durare ore
findings.recommendation: Impostare un timeout massimo di sicurezza es. 3600 secondi anche per le operazioni multi-agente, accoppiato a meccanismi di monitorizzazione.

findings.description: Endpoint hardcoded (HTTP localhost) possono causare profili di rete inefficienti in produzione se l'infrastruttura non è localizzata.
findings.recommendation: Usare variabili d'ambiente o file di configurazione esterni / [FORSE DOVREMMO SCRIVERE QUALI] come richiesto dal Best Practice #2 (Configuration Management).

findings.description: Configuration parameters (trace filter patterns) are hardcoded directly in the code instead of using external configuration files or environment variables.
findings.recommendation: Move GLOBAL_TRACE_EXCLUDE and GLOBAL_TRACE_INCLUDE lists to an external configuration file (e.g., config/settings.py) or use environment variables to comply with the 'Configuration Management' best practice.

findings.description: Logging configuration uses direct 'import logging' inside the configure function, bypassing a dedicated logger instance.
findings.code_snippet:
import logging
logging.info(f"🔍 TRACE FILTERS: Configurati...")
findings.recommendation: Initialize a dedicated logger instance at module level (e.g., logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)) to comply with logging best practices regarding domain and level separation.

findings.description: The main configuration function is defined in the file but has no mechanism to be auto-called or triggered, relying entirely on external logic.
findings.recommendation: Ensure this function is integrated into the main server bootstrapping sequence to guarantee filters are applied when the system starts.

findings.description: Uses string wildcards ('*', '_*') without importing the 'fnmatch' library, assuming 'innertrace' handles string manipulation.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the underlying 'innertrace' library actually supports standard fnmatch wildcards or convert patterns to explicit sets to avoid runtime errors.

findings.description: Hardcoded project_id contains sensitive data that should be in configuration
findings.recommendation: Move project_id to environment variables or configuration file (e.g., .env) to prevent exposure in code

findings.description: Hardcoded type_id violates configuration management best practices
findings.recommendation: Load type_id from configuration file or environment variables for flexibility and maintainability

findings.description: Magic number 0.7 not documented or configurable
findings.recommendation: Define similarity_threshold as configuration parameter with clear documentation

findings.description: Hardcoded IDs make code non-agnostic and tied to specific task
findings.code_snippet: Hardcoded UUIDs for project_id and type_id
findings.recommendability: Extract configurable parameters to config/class_json file for model sharing

findings.description: No graceful degradation if classification service fails
findings.recommendation: Implement graceful degradation with timeout and fallback when dependency fails

findings.description: No exception handling around await call - exceptions silently skipped in debug context
findings.recommendation: Wrap API calls in try-except with logging for better error handling

findings.description: Debug script should validate inputs before processing
findings.recommendation: Add input validation and form handling before calling classification service

findings.description: Valori hardcoded per project_id, type_id e similarity_threshold - dovrebbero essere configurati esternamente
findings.recommendation: Trasferire valori di configurazione in file di ambiente o settings.py

findings.description: Nessun meccanismo di caching per chiamate ripetute con gli stessi parametri, operazione di classificazione potrebbe essere complessa
findings.recommendation: Implementare caching a livello di livello superiore basato su project_id + type_id + similarity_threshold

findings.description: Query a database non monitorate per potenziali performance issues, nessuna indicazione di parametri dinamici
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere database metrics e monitoring per le query correlate alle classiificazioni

findings.description: Output synchronization con print() in una funzione async potrebbe creare bottleneck非terminali
findings.recommendation: Considerare di raccogliere output e scrivere a fine execution o usare async logging

findings.description: I percorsi dei file sono hardcodati. L'uso di una stringa 'datasets' o 'training_dataset.jsonl' fissata nel codice invece di configurazioni noiose viola la best practice di Gestione della Configurazione.
findings.recommendation: Mossa il percorso al base della lista, le impostazioni di progetto in strutture di dati di configurazione o variabili d'ambiente.

findings.description: Valori numerici hardcoded. I definizioni di valori come min/max prezzo (500, 5000) o quantitativi base (100) sono magici e limitano la flessibilità del sistema.
findings.recommendation: Sposta questi parametri in un file di configurazione o usa variabili d'ambiente per permettere modifiche senza toccare il codice.

findings.description: Elenco di oggetti specificati in codice. L'assenza di agnostico al compito richiede una lista di esempi scalabili o icni inflati invece di una lista di ricerca rigidamente specificata.
findings.code_snippet:
sample_objects = real_objects + [
            'Servizio di Consulenza IT',
            'Sviluppo Software su misura',
            ...
findings.recommendation: Cariro questo elenco da una risorsa config o istanza invece di hardcodare le stringhe nel file sorgente per mantenere l'applicazione task-agnostic.

findings.description: Gestione eccezioni generica. L'uso di 'except Exception' senza cattura specifica può nascondere errori specifici e rendere difficile il debug.
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
                    self.logger.warning(f"Errore nel caricare {filename}: {e}")
findings.recommendation: Usa specifiche eccezioni (es. json.JSONDecodeError, FileNotFoundError) per gestire gli errori in modo più chiaro e robusto.

findings.description: Input validation insufficienti. I template Markdown vengono usati senza validazione completa della struttura prima di usare la regex substitution.
findings.recommendation: Implementa una validazione dei template degli input prima dell'elaborazione per evitare runtime errors durante la sostituzione.

findings.description: Sicurezza dei file. Il metodo di scrittura di file non gestisce la creazione del percorso o i permessi appropriati.
findings.recommendation: Verifica che il percorso esista prima di creare il file, assicurandoti di gestire eventuali permessi di scrittura.

findings.description: L'uso di self.project.id nell'unione del percorso sul file system permette potenzialmente di accedere a file al di fuori della directory dei dataset desiderato se l'ID di progetto non è validato o sanitizzato.
findings.recommendation: Usa os.path.realpath e verifica che il percorso finale resti dentro della directory base o valida esplicitamente l'input project.id permettendo solo caratteri alfanumerici.

findings.description: L'uso di os.listdir senza verifica se la directory è esistita o sicura può portare a errori o accesso a risorse non autorizzate se il percorso viene manipolato.
findings.code_snippet: all_files = [f for f in os.listdir(dataset_path) if os.path.isfile(os.path.join(dataset_path, f))]
findings.recommendation: Verifica l'esistenza di dataset_path prima di operare e utilizza os.path.join() combinato con normalization del percorso per evitare directory di fuga.

findings.description: Il parametro target_quantity nella funzione synthesize_data non è validato. Un attaccante potrebbe forzare una quantità molto elevata di generazione, consumando risorse di CPU e memoria.
findings.recommendation: Imposta un limite massimo per target_quantity e valida se è positivo e entro limiti accettabili prima dell'iterazione.

findings.description: Le eccezioni legate al caricamento dei file vengono catturate ma non gestite approfonditamente, rischiando di nascondere tentativi di accesso illegittimo ai file o path.
findings.recommendation: Logga un ID sessione o utente se possibile e indirizza l'errore a un servizio di monitoring per non esporre stack trace sensibili in log pubblici.

findings.description: L'oggetto sintetizzato viene interpolato direttamente in stringhe per regex o prompt senza escaping, potenzialmente rompendo pattern o consentendo iniezioni in strumenti downstream.
findings.recommendation: Applica re.escape su new_object se l'intenzione è trattare il testo come letterale nel pattern, o valida il contenuto prima diinsatz in contromisure.

findings.description: L'uso di self.output_dir senza controllo direzionale potrebbe permettere la scrittura in directory indesiderate se il attributo config è manipolabile.
findings.recommendation: Assicurati che self.output_dir punti a una directory sicura e gestiti per scrittura solo da processi autorizzati.

findings.description: Reading entire file content into memory with f.read() is inefficient for large files. This causes high memory consumption and scaling problems.
findings.code_snippet:
with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
                    content = f.read()
findings.recommendation: Read file line-by-line using a generator pattern with line = f.readline() or iterate for line in f and check each line instead of loading全部内容.
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: Loading entire file into string before searching creates O(file_size) memory usage instead of O(line_size). Large Python files could cause memory pressure.
findings.recommendation: Use iterative line checking: for i, line in enumerate(f): if target_string in line:

findings.description: No caching implemented for repeated scans of the same directory. Config result could be cached.
findings.code_snippet: for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root_dir):
findings.recommendation: Add memoization or caching mechanism for repeated analysis of same target_string and root_dir
findings.reference: Anti-Patterns to Detect - Performance Anti-Patterns - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: String search on entire content may be faster than line-by-line, but for very large files memory usage kills performance gains. Trade-off needs consideration.
findings.recommendation: Consider lazy reading with seeking or process files in chunks if memory is constraint

findings.description: Hardcoded project_id and type_id UUIDs should be externalized to configuration files or environment variables
findings.recommendation: Move these IDs to a configuration file or environment variables for flexibility and security

findings.description: Hardcoded similarity_threshold value should be configurable
findings.recommendation: Define this threshold in a settings file or as an environment variable

findings.description: Multiple hardcoded values violate the "no hardcoded parameters in code" principle
findings.recommendation: Store all configurable values in a centralized configuration module or .env file

findings.description: Missing graceful error handling - appends raise exceptions directly without proper logging or graceful degradation
findings.recommendation: Implement proper exception handling with contextual logging and graceful fallback mechanisms

findings.description: Debug information printed to stdout rather than a proper logging system
findings.recommendation: Use structured logging with domain and severity levels for better production observability

findings.description: Il percorso 'datasets' è hardcoded nel codice invece di essere configurato esternamente.
findings.recommendation: Utilizza la configurazione del progetto o variabili d'ambiente per definire il percorso radice dei dati rispetto al project ID.

findings.description: Numeri magici per la stima della quantità di dati e threshold (3, 10, 0.5, 0.3) non documentati e hardcoded.
findings.recommendation: Migra 'base_examples', 'num_docs_threshold' e 'multiplier' in un file YAML specifici per il modello o il progetto.

findings.description: La lista degli oggetti di esempio è hardcoded, limitando l'uso del synthesizer a task specifici.
findings.code_snippet:
sample_objects = real_objects + [
    "Servizio di Consulenza IT",
    "Sviluppo Software su misura",
    ...
]
findings.recommendation: Passa questa lista come parametro o caricala da un dizionario di configurazione per renderlo task-agnosticic.

findings.description: I limiti di prezzo e grado di variazione sono hardcoded (50, 5000, -0.3, 0.5).
findings.recommendation: Definisci questi parametri in una struttura di configurazione o passali in `config`.

findings.description: Il nome del file di output è hardcoded invece di essere flessibile.
findings.recommendation: Percio il nome del file tramite parametri o configurazione per diverse tipologie di output (es. .csv, .jsonl).

findings.description: Uso di `except Exception` generico anziché imparare su tipi specifici (FileNotFoundError, etc.).
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
    self.logger.warning(f'Errore nel caricare {filename}: {e}')
findings.recommendation: Cattura eccezioni specifiche (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) per avere maggiori dettagli sul contesto specifico dell'errore.

findings.description: Uso di `open` globale senza validazione rigorosa del percorso file per evitare directory traversal o accessi indesiderati.
findings.recommendation: Valida che `file_path` sia contenuto all'interno della directory di fiducia del progetto prima di open().

findings.description: I template regex per estrarre prezzi sono incapsulati nel codice invece di configurati.
findings.recommendation: Estrai i pattern regex in una lista di configurazione per migliorare la manutenibilità.

findings.description: Utilizzo di ID hardcoded (project_id, type_id) direttamente nel codice sorgente, violando il principio di configurazione esterna.
findings.recommendation: Sostituire con variabili d'ambiente o caricare da un file di configurazione (.env o settings.json) all'avvio.

findings.description: Utilizzo di ID hardcoded (type_id) direttamente nel codice sorgente, violando il principio di configurazione esterna.
findings.recommendation: Sostituire con variabili d'ambiente o caricare da un file di configurazione (.env o settings.json) all'avvio.

findings.description: Esposizione di identificatori interni e parametri di configurazione a STDOUT, potenziando possibilità di info leak in log o shell history.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un logger configurato con livelli appropriati o evitare l'output diretto dei dati sensibili in produzione.

findings.description: Possibile esposizione di messaggi di errore relativi all'applicazione in STDOUT.
findings.recommendation: Implementare un gestore di errori opportunato che non esponga stack trace o dettagli sensibili in stderr o log file invece che stdout.

findings.description: La chiamata asincrona manca di trattamento delle eccezioni esplicito, portando a crash non gestiti o eccezioni non loggate correttamente.
findings.recommendation: Includere blocchi try-except che avvoltano la chiamata asincrona per gestire fallimenti gracefulamente e loggare le eccezioni.

findings.description: Function name 'test_execution' is not descriptive and doesn't convey its purpose
findings.recommendation: Rename to descriptive name like 'test_intent_analysis_tool'. This function should clearly indicate it's testing the intent analysis tool.

findings.description: Hardcoded test project_id, user_id, and run_id create tight coupling and should be configurable
findings.code_snippet:
result = await execute_tool(
    tool_name="reflection_intent_analysis",
    arguments={"user_query": user_query, "context": context},
    project_id="test_proj",
    user_id="test_user",
    run_id="test_run"
findings.recommendation: Use configuration variables instead of hardcoded strings. Extract these into a test configuration file or parameters passed to the function.

findings.description: Magic string values used for test parameters make code brittle and harder to maintain
findings.code_snippet:
user_query = "Test query for intent analysis"
    context = {"mock": "context"}
findings.recommendation: Use configuration or parameterize these test values to separate test data from code logic.

findings.description: Logging configured at DEBUG level which generates excessive log noise in production scenarios
findings.recommendation: Set up logging levels based on environment using environment variables rather than hardcoded DEBUG level.

findings.description: Function lacks docstring and type hints, reducing code readability and tooling support
findings.code_snippet:
async def test_execution():
    from utils.tools.tool_registry import execute_tool
findings.recommendation: Add function annotations and docstrings, e.g. 'async def test_intent_analysis(tool_name: str, user_query: str, ...) -> Any:'

findings.description: Global system path manipulation creates unnecessary side effects
findings.recommendation: Remove unnecessary sys.path modifications. If module imports fail, fix the import structure instead of manipulating Python path.

findings.description: Bare exception handler catches all exceptions without proper error handling or specific exception types
findings.recommendation: Use specific exception types or implement proper error handling with context logging as per best practices.

findings.description: Comments should be removed in favor of self-documenting code, inline comments clutter implementation
findings.code_snippet:
# Force discovery
    from utils.tools import tool_registry
findings.recommendation: Consider if code structure would allow proper tool registration on import instead of manual discovery calls.

findings.description: Mixed logging and print statements reduces professional appearance and log aggregation capability
findings.recommendation: Replace all print statements with proper structured logging llamar. Use logger.info(), logger.error(), etc. for consistent log levels.

findings.description: Multiple imports scattered throughout test function rather than at module level
findings.recommendation: Move all imports to the beginning of the module for cleaner organization, unless lazy loading is explicitly required for performance.

findings.description: Full stack trace is printed on exception which could leak internal implementation details about the tool framework and framework structure.
findings.recommendation: Use structured logging with appropriate level and sanitize any internal paths or implementation details before logging
findings.reference: Error Handling: Never expose internal implementation details via logs

findings.description: Hardcoded test values (project_id, user_id, run_id) could expose test infrastructure configuration and be confusing in production if copied
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables for test configuration or keep this file strictly isolated from production code
findings.reference: Configuration Management: NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Test execution doesn't validate or sanitize the user_query input before passing to tool execution, which could propagate vulnerabilities if this pattern is used in production code
findings.recommendation: Add input validation layer for any query parameters before processing, and ensure the underlying tool has proper input sanitization
findings.reference: Security Best Practices: Validate and sanitize all user inputs

findings.description: Hardcoded root directory path with potential syntax error ('r:r:') which will likely prevent the script from correctly traversing the filesystem.
findings.recommendation: Use an environment variable (os.getenv('ROOT_DIR')) or a configuration file to define the scan path dynamically.

findings.description: Specific error string 'Errore nel caricamento models.json' is hardcoded, violating the principle of task agnosticism.
findings.recommendation: Pass this parameter via command line arguments or environment variables to make the script reusable for any search term.

findings.description: Generic Exception handling catches all exceptions, potentially masking specific underlying issues like PermissionError or UnicodeDecodeError.
findings.recommendation: Catch specific exceptions relevant to file operations (e.g., OSError, UnicodeDecodeError) to implement graceful degradation.

findings.description: Reading entire file content into memory using f.read() before iterating is inefficient for large files.
findings.recommendation: Use f.read() only if line iteration isn't used; prefer iterating line by line to search for the target string directly to save memory.

findings.description: The 'errors=ignore' argument in open() suppresses decoding errors, which can lead to data corruption or loss of context.
findings.code_snippet: with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
findings.recommendation: Use 'errors='replace'' to log where decoding fails, or validate encoding expectations before reading.

findings.description: No validation that the root directory exists before starting the recursion.
findings.code_snippet: for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root_dir):
findings.recommendation: Add os.path.exists() and os.path.isdir() checks before walking to prevent RuntimeErrors on invalid paths.

findings.description: Silent exception swallowing prevents detection of startup failures. The function passes silently if import fails, potentially causing undefined behavior later.
findings.recommendation: Log the failure using logged.error() and re-raise or handle with a factory pattern to ensure critical dependencies are available on startup.
findings.reference: Best Practice #4: Error Handling - Never silently swallow exceptions

findings.description: Filter patterns are hardcoded in Python code instead of using a configuration file or environment variables. This violates configuration management best practices.
findings.recommendation: Move these filter lists to an external config file (e.g., config/trace_config.yaml) and load them at startup to allow external modification without recompiling code.
findings.reference: Best Practice #2: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Modules are imported inside functions instead of at module level. This can impact import speed and log performance.
findings.recommendation: Move `import logging` to the top of the file (line 1 after header) for consistency and better performance.
findings.reference: Best Practice #2: Configuration Management - Use best practices for imports

findings.description: Specific module name 'innertrace' is hardcoded. If the tracing architecture changes, this file becomes fragile.
findings.recommendation: Abstract the trace module access in a configuration object or use a base interface to allow swapping the tracing engine.
findings.reference: Best Practice #1: Code Agnosticism - Avoid hardcoding specific llms/module names

findings.description: Use of Unicode emojis in comments and docstrings may cause encoding issues in specific logging systems or pipelines.
findings.recommendation: Use standard ASCII characters for inline comments and identifiers in strict environments, though this is not critical for standard Python 3 environments.
findings.reference: Best Practice #3: System Prompts - Avoid project-specific context in system level (generalizes to code readability)

findings.description: Root directory path is hardcoded in code instead of using environment variables or configuration file (Best Practice 2).
findings.recommendation: Move root_dir to a config file or environment variable.

findings.description: Search target string is hardcoded which makes the script project-specific and reduces agnosticism (Best Practice 1).
findings.recommendation: Allow search pattern to be configured via parameters or config file.

findings.description: File reading uses errors='ignore' which silently swallows encoding errors, violating exception handling best practices (Best Practice 4).
findings.code_snippet: with open(filepath, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
findings.recommendation: Remove errors='ignore' to properly detect and log encoding issues.

findings.description: Generic Exception catch prevents specific error diagnostics and debugging (Best Practice 4).
findings.recommendation: Catch specific exceptions like PermissionError or FileNotFoundError and log specific context.

findings.description: Importation of modules inside async function may cause repeated module loading overhead if function is called multiple times. Modules should be imported at module level.
findings.recommendation: Move imports to top of file

findings.description: Logging level set to DEBUG unnecessarily in production. Verbose logging increases I/O operations and can impact performance.
findings.recommendation: Configure logging level as environment variable

findings.description: Multiple print() statements used instead of structured logging. This can lead to inconsistent logging and potential I/O overhead.
findings.code_snippet: print(f"Executing tool reflection_intent_analysis directly via execute_tool...")
findings.recommendation: Replace print() statements with structured logging using logger

findings.description: Project name hardcoded instead of configurable
findings.recommendation: Use only environment variable, no default hardcoded value, or make it a parameter

findings.description: Default model name hardcoded
findings.recommendation: Remove default hardcoded model name, use only environment variable or parameter

findings.description: Generic exception handling without specific exception types
findings.recommendation: Catch specific exceptions (e.g., ConnectionError, RateLimitError) instead of generic Exception

findings.description: Evaluation not gracefully degraded on failure
findings.code_snippet:
except Exception as e:
    logger.error(f"❌ Evaluation error: {e}", exc_info=True)
findings.recommendation: Implement retry logic or cache fallback when LangSmith is unreachable

findings.description: Magic number for limit parameter
findings.recommendation: Make limit configurable via environment variable or parameter

findings.description: Function name exposed through debug logging
findings.recommendation: Use stable, non-revealing names instead of exposing implementation details

findings.description: Emoji characters in log messages
findings.recommendation: Remove emojis from log messages for structured logging compatibility

findings.description: Limited error context in logging
findings.recommendation: Include domain and log level parameters for structured logging

findings.description: Missing docstrings on public utility functions
findings.recommendation: Add docstrings to enable_dev_eval() and disable_dev_eval()

findings.description: No input validation on user-provided values
findings.recommendation: Validate model names to prevent injection attacks or invalid configurations

findings.description: Il percorso della directory di ricerca (root_dir) è hardcoded nel codice, rendendo l'applicazione non configurabile e non portabile.
findings.recommendation: Sposta il percorso in una variabile di ambiente (os.getenv) o in un file di configurazione esterno.

findings.description: La stringa di ricerca (target_string) è hardcoded e non gestibile senza modificare il codice sorgente.
findings.recommendation: Definisci target_string come variabile in ingresso o da file di config.

findings.description: L'eccessivo uso di print() per il logging intrinseco riduce il controllo del processo di debug e le performance.
findings.recommendation: Sostituisci print con la libreria logging standardizzata del linguaggio.

findings.description: L'handlign delle eccezioni con 'except Exception' è troppo generico e nasconde dettagli utili per il debugging.
findings.recommendation: Cattura eccezioni specifiche (FileNotFoundError, UnicodeError) o usa logging a livello appropriato.

findings.description: L'uso di errors="ignore" nell'apertura del file può nascondere critici errori di codifica.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovi errors="ignore" e gestisci correttamente le eccezioni di decodifica per rilevare bug affidabili.

findings.description: Le variabili target_string e root_dir hanno ambiguità nel naming convention standard per costanti o parametri interni.
Model ID 'qwen2.5-14b-instruct' is hardcoded multiple times instead of using environment variables or config files
findings.recommendation: Move model configuration to .env file and use environment variables for model selection

findings.description: Model ID 'deepseek-chat' is hardcoded instead of being configurable
findings.recommendation: Load available models from configuration file and reference them by name

findings.description: Project name 'personaltune-agents' is hardcoded throughout multiple example functions
findings.recommendation: Make project name configurable via parameter or environment variable

findings.description: qwen2.5-14b-instruct model name appears 3 times, indicating code duplication
findings.recommendation: Create a constant or config object for model names to avoid repetition

findings.description: Magic number text[:50] used for truncation without explanation
findings.recommendation: Define MAX_TRUNCATE_LENGTH constant or add configuration for truncation behavior

findings.description: Mixed sync and async functions in example_9 without clear separation boundary
findings.recommendation: Clearer separation between sync examples and async examples, or use documentation to mark async usage

findings.description: Example functions return None with no explicit documentation that functions are for demonstration only
findings.recommendation: Add docstring explicitly stating 'Example function for demonstration purposes'

findings.description: list_recent_runs called with limit=5 without import validation
findings.recommendation: Add parameter validation and check if limit parameter is enforced in the imported function

findings.description: Model IDs are hardcoded in multiple locations (Examples 1-10), preventing model-agnostic implementation
findings.recommendation: Move model_id to a configuration file or environment variable like models.json for model-agnostic design

findings.description: Project name 'personaltune-agents' is hardcoded multiple times throughout the examples
findings.recommendation: Use environment variable for project_name to allow configuration through .env file

findings.description: Multiple sections reference 'personaltune-agents' but Application should be task-agnostic
findings.code_snippet: evaluating runs with hardcoded project name
findings.recommendation: Introduction should abstract project names into configuration

findings.description: Version strings like 'v1', 'qwen_v1', 'deepseek_v1' are hardcoded
findings.recommendation: Environment should define model version parameters

findings.description: list_recent_runs(limit=5) uses hardcoded limit
findings.recommendation: Allow limit configuration via environment variable or parameter

findings.description: Heavy use of print statements instead of logging system
findings.recommendation: Delegate to logger for consistent logging with domain and level parameters

findings.description: No error handling in batch_evaluation function
findings.recommendation: Implement graceful degradation when dependencies fail

findings.description: Main runner contains repetitive print statements for each example
findings.recommendation: Use a helper function to generate example separators and reduce repetition

findings.description: Docstrings lack examples and clear context for each evaluator type
findings.recommendation: Enhance docstrings with use case examples and expected parameters

findings.description: Comments are mixed with docstrings - some important
findings.recommendation: Separate actual code comments from docstring content for clarity

findings.description: Hardcoded identifiers (project_id, user_id) reduce portability and can introduce security issues if used with real data.
findings.recommendation: Load identifiers from environment variables or a secure configuration store via startup settings.

findings.description: Hardcoded test arguments and tool names couple the script to internal tooling that may change.
findings.recommendation: Pass tool names and arguments via configuration parameters or command line args.

findings.description: Hardcoded sys.path modification can cause import confusion and risk of loading unintended modules.
findings.recommendation: Use a proper virtual environment or package manager (uv/venv/pip) instead of modifying sys.path.

findings.description: Using print() and traceback.print_exc() instead of the logger bypasses centralized logging and filtering rules.
findings.recommendation: Use logger.exception(e) to maintain structured logs consistent with the application logging configuration.

findings.description: No caching mechanism for model evaluations - repeated evaluations of same input will waste compute resources and increase costs
findings.recommendation: Implement LRU or TTL caching for evaluation results to avoid re-evaluating identical inputs. Consider using a cache key based on input, evaluator type, and criteria combination.
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: Example 9 shows async usage but the actual Lambda function in main() runs sync evaluation - inconsistent async/sync usage pattern
findings.recommendation: Ensure async evaluation functions in production code are called where the underlying evaluation operations would benefit from true async handling (e.g., concurrent LLM calls). Consider if sync wrapper should run asyncio.run() or be restructured.

findings.description: Main function creates multiple evaluators repeatedly without consideration for resource reuse - could lead to duplicate model initializations
findings.code_snippet:
def main():
    # Run examples
    example_1_basic_evaluator()
    example_7_batch_evaluation()
findings.recommendation: Create a singleton or cached evaluator factory to reuse model instances across example calls where possible. Consider creating evaluators once and passing them to example functions.
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: No cleanup of temporary or large objects - print statements create temporary string objects that are never garbage collected efficiently
findings.recommendation: While Python handles this automatically, consider using context managers or dedicated display utility for large outputs. Reuse string patterns instead of creating new ones repeatedly.
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: Valori hardcoded come nome del modello e nome del progetto violano il principio di Agnosticismo del codice (Best Practice #1). Il codice dovrebbe essere riutilizzabile su altri modelli/progetti.
findings.recommendation: Sposta questi valori in un file di configurazione esteriore o file .env wildcard (--config)

findings.description: Ambiente predefinito hardcoded con nome specifico 'personaltune-agents' fragilmente legato a un contesto specifico, prendendo standard configurazione per cu cost
findings.recommendation: Usa un'inizializzazione centralizzata per la configurazione con naming costante in utils

findings.description: Uso eccessivo di emoji nei log (tweak) può ridurre la legibilità nei sistemi di log strutturati e riduce il commit del debug non Tecnico.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere caratteri grafici emoji dai messaggi di log e mantenersi un chiaro livello

findings.description: Modifica dello stato globale tramite funzioni ('enable_dev_eval') mutabilizza la configurazione durante l'esecuzione, violando il principio di gestione della configurazione all'avvio.
findings.recommendation: Evitare la manipolazione diretta di variabili globali di stato; usare classi o context manager per la gestione del contesto

findings.description: Uso di 'traceable' su funzioni che possono essere asincrone, corpo implementato. Richiede attenzione se sync/async non feed properly applicato.
findings.recommendation: Verificare se la decostrazione 'traceable' supporta correttamente funzioni asincrone senza wrapping esplicito

findings.description: API key hardcoded in environment dict will be logged in traces and potentially sent to LLM provider, creating security and performance risks
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to .env file and load via environment variables. Never log or pass secrets to LLM providers.

findings.description: Only 7 lines of code (excluding comments and blank lines), no algorithmic complexity concerns for this demo
findings.recommendation: Code complexity is acceptable for demo purposes. Keep as-is for demonstration clarity.

findings.description: No caching implemented for LLM responses - if this script runs multiple times, same queries will be repeated
findings.code_snippet: llm_response = "The answer is 4. This is basic arithmetic addition of two plus two."
findings.recommendation: Consider implementing a simple cache for LLM responses to avoid redundant API calls in production.

findings.description: Multiple print statements for debugging will generate unnecessary I/O overhead in production scenarios
findings.recommendation: Collect debug output to a central logging system instead of multiple print statements. Only log at appropriate levels.

findings.description: LLM calls and tool operations should be async for better performance with multiple concurrent operations
findings.recommendation: Use async/await for LLM and tool calls to enable concurrent execution and reduce total execution time.

findings.description: Chiamata a blocco di I/O sincrona (`_evaluate_last_run`) all'interno di una funzione asincrona (`_run_and_eval_async`), che blocca il loop degli eventi e riduce le prestazioni su larga scala.
findings.recommendation: Rendere `_evaluate_last_run` asincrono (`async def`) e chiamarlo con `await` per non bloccare il thread asincrono, oppure isolare il lavoro di valutazione in bathrut.

findings.description: Creazione di un nuovo istanza `Client()` di LangSmith in ogni chiamata a `_evaluate_last_run`, generando overhead diestablishment HTTP/Network connections per ogni valutazione.
findings.recommendation: Creare un singleton o condividere il client a livello globale/app per evitare di ri-iniziare la connessione per ogni singola valutazione.

findings.description: Creazione di una lista (`runs = list(...)`) per ogni chiamata di valutazione senza pulizia esplicita dell'Memory, creando pressione su oggetti 'orphan' a lungo termine.
findings.recommendation: Implementare un garbage collection esplicito o chiudere le stream basate sulla fine della sessione specifica, anche se ListsPythonDiGa volta.

findings.description: Coduct without caching of configuration values (like `DEV_EVAL_MODEL`), forcing re-fetch/lookup logic repeatedly even if frequent.
findings.recommendation: Impostare il modello di valutazione come variabile globale caching in fase di init per evitare lookup ripetuti se non necessario.

findings.description: Uso di `asyncio.run()` all'interno di `_run_and_eval_async` se l'ambiente di esecuzione è già asincrono, creating nested event loops che possono causare sta della throw in errori di runtime.
findings.recommendation: Impostare `run_and_eval` per essere senza `asyncio.run()` se chiamato in un contesto asincrono, o iniettare il `loop` come argomento.

findings.description: API key is hardcoded in source code which exposes credentials to version control, logging, and anyone with access to the file
findings.recommendation: Move sensitive credentials to environment variables using os.getenv() or a secure secrets manager. Example: env={"api_key": os.getenv("API_KEY")}

findings.description: LLM response containing user data and trace results are printed to stdout, potentially exposing sensitive client information in logs or logs in serverless environments
findings.recommendation: Avoid printing sensitive user data or LLM responses to stdout. Only log non-sensitive trace metadata for monitoring purposes

findings.description: Generic Exception handler catches all exceptions without specific type handling, making debugging less precise and potentially hiding important error details
findings.recommendation: Use specific exception types or ensure the emit_exception method properly serializes the full exception data including type, message, and traceback

findings.description: User-provided arguments like 'query', 'user_id' and tool parameters are used without sanitization, potential vulnerability if this code processes external user input
findings.recommendation: Implement input validation and sanitization for all external inputs. Validate user_id format, sanitize query parameters before processing

findings.description: Hardcoded API_URL with HTTP instead of HTTPS on production
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS in production or make URL configurable via environment variables

findings.description: Repeated API_URL in both functions violates DRY principle
findings.recommendation: Extract configuration to shared settings or use constants module

findings.description: Hardcoded model_id 'gpt-3.5-turbo' should be configurable
findings.recommendation: Configure model via environment variable or config file

findings.description: Hardcoded demo user/project IDs in code
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or configuration file for these values

findings.description: Missing request timeout specification (risk of hanging connection)
findings.recommendation: Add 'timeout' parameter to avoid hanging connections indefinitely

findings.description: Duplicate request payload structure in both stream and non-stream functions
findings.code_snippet:
def stream_chat_example():
    ... Response payload structure repeated at line 40
findings.recommendation: Create a shared payload builder function or class

findings.description: Magic number '6' for decoding 'data: ' prefix not explained
findings.recommendation: Define as constant PREFIX_LENGTH = 6 or use f'{separator}:' parse method

findings.description: Silent exception handler catches JSONDecodeError and passes without logging
findings.recommendation: Add logging for debugging or raise appropriate exception

findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy or authentication headers in request
findings.recommendation: Add auth headers if required by API (e.g., Authorization: Bearer <token>)

findings.description: function names could be more descriptive
findings.recommendation: Use more descriptive names like 'perform_streaming_chat_request()' or 'perform_batch_chat_request()'

findings.description: No connection pool timeout or max retries configured
findings.recommendation: Consider using httpx or session-based requests with retry logic

findings.description: Missing __all__ export definition for module clarity
findings.recommendation: Consider adding __all__ if this is meant to be a utility module

findings.description: API URL hardcoded as constant value
findings.recommendation: Move API_URL to configuration file or environment variables

findings.description: Model ID hardcoded in request payload
findings.recommendation: Use parameter or environment variable for model selection

findings.description: No connection timeout specified in HTTP requests
findings.recommendation: Add timeout parameter to prevent hanging requests

findings.description: Missing retry logic for network failures
findings.recommendation: Implement retry mechanism with exponential backoff for transient network errors

findings.description: Hardcoded demo context values in payload
findings.recommendation: Make context values configurable through parameters or environment variables

findings.description: Print statements instead of proper logging
findings.recommendation: Replace print() calls with proper logging module with category and level parameters

findings.description: No rate limiting on streaming requests
findings.recommendation: Implement rate limiting to prevent overwhelming the streaming endpoint

findings.description: Missing context manager for HTTP responses
findings.recommendation: Use proper resource cleanup with try/finally for response handling

findings.description: No HTTPS used for external communication (localhost only but should be enforced)
findings.recommendation: Consider using HTTPS for production environments, validate host in production

findings.description: Duplicate API_URL variable in non_stream_chat_example
findings.recommendation: Share API configuration between functions to avoid duplication

findings.description: flush=True called on each print but no line limiting could cause heavy output
findings.recommendation: Buffer output in batches to reduce I/O overhead for large content

findings.description: Chiave API hardcoded (sk-secret123) Violazione della sicurezza.
findings.recommendation: Sposta la chiave API nel file .env e carica un valore tramite environment variable (es. os.getenv('API_KEY')).

findings.description: Modello LLM hardcoded in codice invece che nella configurazione.
findings.recommendation: Utilizza un modello configurabile o ambiente per la configurazione del modello (es. config.json o variazioni da .env).

findings.description: Logging non strutturato per debug, utilizzo di print standard.
findings.recommendation: Implementa un sistema di logging strutturato appropriato per evitare dipendenze da stderr/stdout per mantere l'audit di qualitative.

findings.description: Manipolazioni del path di sistema direttamente nello script.
findings.recommendation: Evita修改 del path del sistema negli script principali per garantire portabilità in ambienti produttivi.

findings.description: Assenza di riferimento a documentazione architetturale esterna.
findings.recommendation: Riferisci le funzionalit nella cartella architecture/ come richiesto dalle best practice.

findings.description: No authentication token or API key included in request headers. The client sends requests to the API without proper authentication, which could expose the client to unauthorized access.
findings.recommendation: Add Authorization header with Bearer token or API key for identity verification. Reference: Best Practice #5 - Security Best Practices

findings.description: Using HTTP instead of HTTPS for API communication. This exposes all data in transit to potential interception and man-in-the-middle attacks.
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS for all external communications. Set API_URL to "https://localhost:5000/api/chat_v2" or configure proper SSL context. Reference: Best Practice #5 - Security Best Practices

findings.description: User context values (demo-user, demo-project) are hardcoded in the code. In production, these should be configurable via environment variables.
findings.recommendation: Move user_id and project_id to environment variables or configuration file. Reference: Best Practice #2 - Configuration Management

findings.description: API_URL, model_id, and other configuration values are hardcoded. These should be externalized to settings files or environment variables.
findings.recommendation: Configure API_URL, model_id, and other sensitive values through environment variables or a configuration file. Reference: Best Practice #2 - Configuration Management

findings.description: No validation of user input (message content) before sending to the API. Malicious input could be processed by the backend without sanitization checks.
findings.code_snippet: "message": "Write a short story about a robot learning to paint.",
findings.recommendation: Implement input validation and sanitization for user messages before sending. Reference: Best Practice #5 - Security Best Practices (Validate and sanitize all user inputs)

findings.description: The requests library is used directly without proper dependency injection. This makes testing and mocking difficult and couples the code to a specific library.
findings.recommendation: Implement dependency injection with an interface/abstract class for HTTP client to allow testing and flexibility. Reference: Best Practice #1 - Code Agnosticism

findings.description: Security-sensitive error messages expose potentially useful information. Error details may leak about system structure.
findings.recommendation: Implement proper error handling with structured logging (domain + level) without exposing internal implementation details. Reference: Best Practice #4 - Error Handling

findings.description: Hardcoded API key in code violates security best practices. Sensitive values must never be stored directly in source code.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables (e.g., os.environ.get) or a secure configuration management tool like a secrets vault.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Sensitive values (API keys, passwords) should NEVER be in code, but pnly in file .env

findings.description: Hardcoded LLM model name restricts flexibility. Model configuration should be externalized.
findings.recommendation: Pass the model name as a parameter or load it from a configuration file or environment variable.
findings.reference: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Modifying sys.path directly in source code is discouraged. It relies on specific project structure and complicates dependency management.
findings.recommendation: Use proper package installation or set PYTHONPATH via environment variables. Structure imports as proper Python packages.
findings.reference: Configuration Management - Use environment variables for environment-specific configuration

findings.description: Full exception traceback is logged using exc_info=True, potentially exposing internal application paths, function names and implementation details.
findings.recommendation: Remove exc_info=True or implement custom exception handling that logs sanitized stack traces. Log the error message separately.

findings.presentation: Hardcoded default project name repeated in code.
findings.recommendation: Use a single configuration file (e.g., settings.py) and references for default values to maintain consistency and auditability.

findings.presentation: Configuration values exposed in console output during demo.
findings.recommendation: Hide internal configuration values from stdout during testing/demo. Remove debug prints before production deployment.

findings.description: Third-party metadata from LangSmith evaluation is stored without sanitization or control.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or restrict allowed fields in metadata before passing to create_feedback to prevent storing unexpected user data or injection risks.

findings.description: Accumulo di tutti i dati sintetizzati nella lista 'synthesized_data' prima della scrittura sul file. Con un target_quantity elevato (>20k), questo causa picchi di memoria RAM.
findings.recommendation: Scrivere ogni item direttamente nel file in streaming (f.write + flush) invece di accumularli in memoria.

findings.description: Uso di esepresioni Regex (re.sub) all'interno del ciclo principale di generazione. Ogni iterazione rilancia sovraccarito CPU.
findings.recommendation: Precorreggere il template e compilare i regex pattern una volta sola fuori dalla funzione/loop.

findings.description: Operazioni di I/O su file (open/read/write) sono sincrono e bloccanti. In un ambiente Web, questo blocca il thread visitor.
findings.code_snippet:
with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare operazioni async (es. aiofiles) per I/O non bloccante durante il parsing dei documenti.

findings.description: Parsing testuale con Regex per estrarre oggetti e prezzi. Logica divisionale è ridondante per ogni documento.
findings.recommendation: Considerare un parser più strutturato (es. cl/lexer) o pre-processing con regex statiche.

findings.description: I nomi dei modelli e del progetto sono hardcoded nel codice. Questo viola le best practice di gestione della configurazione (Best Practice #2) e fa sì che dati interni (nome progetto) siano visibili in file source code, potenzialmente sollecitando info disclosure.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare variabili d'ambiente o un file di configurazione esterno (es. config/config.py) per il nome del progetto e dei modelli, come suggerito nel testo del file stesso.

findings.description: Il file stampa messaggi che espongono dettagli interni come `domain: code_generation` o configurazioni dei modelli, che potrebbero essere utili per attacker durante l'OSINT.
findings.recommendation: Limitare la stampa di dettagli di configurazione in file di esempio o rimovgere debug log in produzione.

findings.description: Lightweight models are re-initialized on every request via choose_model() - should be cached/shared across requests
findings.recommendation: Move model initialization to setup() or implement model caching to avoid re-loading weights on every request

findings.description: No caching of image preprocessing (resize operations are expensive GPU operations)
findings.code_snippet:
if h < 300:
                img = cv2.resize(img, (w * 2, h * 2), interpolation=cv2.INTER_LANCZOS4)
findings.recommendation: Implement caching for image transformations and downsample settings to avoid repeated expensive GPU operations

findings.description: Multiple os.system() calls for model downloads and pip install create blocking operations that delay request processing
findings.code_snippet:
os.system('pip install gfpgan')
os.system('python setup.py develop')
findings.recommendation: Replace os.system() calls with async subprocess or background worker for dependency installation

findings.description: Temporary files created without centralized cleanup tracking - temp folder operations in finally block may not execute if predict() raises before model loading
findings.recommendation: Implement proper resource cleanup with try-finally earlier in execution flow, track file handles for explicit closing

findings.description: All I/O operations (file reads/writes, model ops) are synchronous - no async/await optimization available for concurrent requests
findings.recommendation: Convert to async with Prefect or FastAPI async handlers for concurrent request handling, use torch with async loading

findings.description: Model instance stored as self.upsampler without explicit cleanup - potential memory leak across multiple requests
findings.recommendation: Implement model instance cleanup in teardown() or explicitly clean up tensor references after processing

findings.description: Concatenazione di stringhe inefficiente. Appendere stringhe in un loop aumenta l'allocation di memoria e la complessità temporale per stringhe lunghe.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare io.StringIO per accumulare, o scrivere direttamente su file/stream se la dimensione è molto grande, per evitare copie multiple.

findings.description: Utilizzo di un client HTTP sincro (blocking) per operazioni di streaming. Limita il throughput e impedisce la multitasking effettiva in ambiente produttivo.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzerebbe `httpx` o `aiohttp` per supportare operazioni asincrone e non bloccare il thread principale durante il download dei dati.

findings.description: Operazioni di sottomissione delle stringhe (slicing) in ogni iterazione generano nuovi oggetti di stringa, aumentando la pressione sui garbage collector.
findings.recommendation: Ottimizzare la parsing logic utilizzando deserializzazione binaria dove possibile o ridurre la quantità di stringhe intermedie create nel ciclo.

findings.description: Use of os.system() for dependency installation. This is a security risk and bad practice. Should use import or proper package management (e.g., torch.cuda.is_available() for checking)
findings.recommendation: Remove os.system calls. Dependencies should be in requirements.txt and installed via virtual environment
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Use proper authentication, sanitize inputs, use parameterized queries

findings.description: Using os.system() is dangerous and can expose system to attacks. Should use proper module imports
findings.recommendation: Use proper package imports instead of system calls
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Hardcoded credentials, os.system vulnerability

findings.description: Hardcoded URLs for model weights in multiple locations. Should be externalized to configuration
findings.recommendation: Move model paths and URLs to external configuration file (e.g., model_config.json or .env file)
findings.reference: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Multiple repeated os.system() calls with wget URLs hardcoded. Poor architecture, should use proper HTTP client with error handling
findings.recommendation: Create a dedicated module for model download with proper exception handling and logging
findings.reference: Error Handling - Use proper exception handling, implement graceful degradation

findings.description: Repeated model weight download pattern (4-5 similar code blocks). Violates DRY principle
findings.recommendation: Extract into a separate function like 'download_model_if_needed(model_name, url, file_path)'
findings.reference: Code Quality - Avoid code duplication (DRY principle)

findings.code_snippet:
if tile <= 100 or tile is None:
            tile = 0
findings.description: Magic numbers used (100, 0) without explanation. Threshold logic should be configurable
findings.recommendation: Define TILE_MIN_THRESHOLD = 100 in configuration section

findings.description: Identical RealESRGANer initialization pattern repeated 4 times in choose_model. Deep code nesting
findings.recommendation: Use a dictionary or switch-case pattern to reduce nesting and avoid duplication
findings.reference: Code Quality - Deep nesting, Code duplication

findings.description: Only printing runtime errors. No proper logging with domain/type/level. Silent exceptions
findings.recommendation: Use proper logging module with structured logging: logger.error('Image processing failed for image: {img_id}, model: {model}', extra={'level': 'ERROR', 'domain': 'image_processing'})
findings.reference: Error Handling - Log errors with sufficient context for debugging

findings.description: Catching all exceptions with bare except Exception as error. No specific exception types, limits are too broad
findings.recommendation: Use specific exception types and implement proper exception hierarchy
findings.reference: Error Handling - Use proper exception handling with specific exception types

findings.code_snippet:
def clean_folder(folder):
    for filename in os.listdir(folder):
findings.description: Module-level function at bottom of file with no documentation. Should have clear docstring
findings.recommendation: Add docstring and place utility functions in separate utils.py module for better organization
findings.reference: Documentation - Write self-documenting code with clear function names

findings.description: Flake8 suppress is used on entire file, prevents linting errors from being fixed
findings.recommendation: Remove flake8: noqa and fix issues directly, or use it selectively per line/block

findings.description: Missing docstring for predict method. Public methods should document parameters and return values
findings.recommendation: Add comprehensive docstring describing input parameters, output, and behavior
findings.reference: Documentation - Add docstrings to all public functions and classes

findings.description: API Key is hardcoded directly in source code instead of using environment variables or secrets management.
findings.recommendation: Remove the hardcoded value 'secret123' and pass the API key via environment variables or secure configuration files.

findings.description: Internal run ID and system latency are exposed in stdout output which could leak sensitive internal state or timing information.
findings.recommendation: Avoid printing internal system identifiers and timing to stdout. Use structured logging to a local file or dedicated monitoring system instead.

findings.description: Generic exception handler prints error details directly to output, potentially exposing internal stack traces or error messages.
findings.recommendation: Implement a logging mechanism that filters sensitive content before outputting to the console.

findings.description: Segreti sensibili hardcoded. L'invio di chiave API (api_key) direttamente nel codice viola le best practice di gestione della configurazione.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare un file d'.env o variabili d'ambiente per le credenziali e leggerele all'avvio dell'applicazione.

findings.description: Model name hardcoded. L'uso di 'gpt-4' direttamente nel codice della chiamata LLM riduce la portabilità e itala gestione dei costi/modelli future.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare una configurazione esterna per definire il modello LLM predefinito invece di hardcodarlo nello script.

findings.description: Modifica manuale di sys.path. Modificare sys.path può causare conflitti di import e rendere difficile la manutenzione del progetto.
findings.recommendation: Strutturare il progetto come un pacchetto installabile o usare import relativi (`..`) definiti rispetto agli standard setuptools.

findings.description: Manca di type hints. Le funzioni `main` e `fake_llm_call` non hanno annotazioni di tipo, rendendo più difficile refactoring e verifica statica.
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere type hints a tutte le funzioni pubbliche e private seguendo PEP 484 (opcionalmente con Callable).

findings.description: Utilizzo di `print` per diagnosticazione invece che logging strutturato. Manca di contesto e livello definiti nel log.
findings.recommendation: Sostituire `print` con il modulo `logging` per registrare eventi con livello e dominio consistenti.

findings.description: Hardcoded URLs and paths for model downloads scattered throughout the code instead of externalized configuration
findings.code_snippet:
for url in file_url:
    # model_path will be updated
    model_path = load_file_from_url(
        url=url, model_dir=os.path.join(ROOT_DIR, 'weights'), progress=True, file_name=None)
findings.recommendation: Move all model URLs and feature flags to configuration files (e.g., models.json or settings.py)
findings.reference: Configuration Management - NO hardcoded parameters in code

findings.description: Long function with ~250 lines containing multiple responsibilities (argument parsing, model selection, file handling, inference loop)
findings.recommendation: Split into smaller functions: ModelConfiguration, FileProcessing, FaceEnhancement, ImageInference, respectively
findings.reference: Code Quality - Keep functions small and focused (single responsibility)

findings.description: GFPGAN model path hardcoded as string literal instead of configurable value
findings.recommendation: Move to environment variable or configuration file for GFPGAN model location
findings.reference: Configuration Management - sensitive values and paths should never be in code

findings.description: API keys and sensitive configuration mixed with runtime logic; should separate external model sources
findings.code_snippet:
from realesrgan import RealESRGANer
from realesrgan.archs.srvgg_arch import SRVGGNetCompact
findings.recommendation: Separate external dependencies configuration into dedicated config section with clear separation of concerns
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Sensitive values should only be in file .env

findings.description: Generic error handling with print statements instead of structured logging with domain and level
findings.recommendation: Implement logging system with domain and level parameters for automatic filtering and contextual debugging
findings.reference: Error Handling - Log errors with sufficient context for debugging

findings.description: Long parameter string in argparse help text (lines 19-25) making it difficult to maintain and understand
findings.code_snippet:
help=('Model names: RealESRGAN_x4plus | RealESRNet_x4plus | RealESRGAN_x4plus_anime_6B | RealESRGAN_x2plus | '
        'realesr-animevideov3 | realesr-general-x4v3'))
findings.recommendation: Use separate helper function for help text, or split into multiple files; keep it maintainable and testable
findings.reference: Code Quality - Write self-documenting code with clear function/method names

findings.description: Multiple nested conditional blocks for model selection create 'feature envy' pattern where a single block does too much
findings.recommendation: Create a configuration dictionary or dataclass for model options and validate with isinstance checks
findings.reference: Code Smells - Feature envy and avoid code duplication (DRY principle)

findings.description: Inconsistency in path handling - sometimes using args.path, sometimes relative paths without clear documentation
findings.recommendation: Use consistent path handling throughout; consider using Path objects from pathlib or a PathManager class
findings.reference: Code Quality - Use consistent naming conventions

findings.description: No caching mechanism for file operations; repeated reads if same model is processed multiple times
findings.recommendation: Consider caching model weights and add simple file-level caching for repeated image processing
findings.reference: Performance Anti-Patterns - Missing caching for expensive operations

findings.description: Magic numbers: netscale=4, netyscale=2, num_block=23, num_conv=16 hardcoded throughout model configuration
findings.recommendation: Extract model parameters to configuration object rather than hardcoding numbers in if blocks

findings.description: Il contesto di tracing non viene necessariamente chiuso. La rubrica 'end_traced_run' è chiamata solo all'interno del block 'except'. Se l'esecuzione va a buon fine senza errori, il run finisce senza una corretta chiusura dello span, portando possibile a context leaks o risorse di memory non rilasciate dal tracing system.
findings.recommendation: Muovi le strutture di logical exit e cleanup in un blocco 'finally' esterno al 'try' or asegura che end_traced_run venga chiamato sempre indipendentemente dall'outcome.

findings.description: L'uso di lambda per avvolgere funzioni asincrone.
findings.recommendation: Passa il coroutine direttamente se supportato dal tracer, o assegna il risultato direttamente. Il lambda crea un overhead di closure non necessario.

findings.description: Misurazione della latenza con 'time.time()' non è la più precisa per eventi interni che durano substrul microsecondi.
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare time.perf_counter() per maggiore precisione nella misurazione del tempo di esecuzione.

findings.description: Mancanza di caching per chiamate espensive (es. LLM). Se l'applicazione chiama la stessa query di testi/LLM frequentemente, si consuma tempo di rete e risorse CPU per密不可分 di risultato.
findings.recommendation: Implementare un cache layer (LRU) per chiavi di richieste simili per ridurre le chiamate a LLM ridondanti.

findings.description: The args.input parameter is used directly to construct paths without validation, allowing potential path traversal attacks (e.g., ../../etc/passwd)
findings.recommendation: Validate the input path to ensure it doesn't contain path traversal sequences. Use path normalization and verify the resolved path is within an expected root directory

findings.description: The args.output directory is used directly without validation, allowing users to specify arbitrary directories including symlinks to sensitive locations
findings.recommendation: Validate that the output path is absolute or within a safe base directory. Prevent directory traversal by resolving and checking the canonical path

findings.description: Output filename is constructed directly from user input (imgname) without sanitization, potentially allowing special characters that could interfere with file handling
findings.recommendation: Sanitize filename components by removing or escaping special characters. Consider using a fixed naming convention and validate allowed characters

findings.description: Runtime errors expose internal error details that could leak stack traces or internal state information
findings.recommendation: Catch and log errors internally with appropriate context for debugging, but show only user-friendly messages to the user

findings.description: GFPGAN model download URL is hardcoded, creating a supply chain dependency that cannot be easily updated or switched
findings.recommendation: Move external URLs to a configuration file or environment variable. Allow runtime configuration of remote model sources

findings.description: No file extension validation prevents users from selecting unexpected output formats that might not be supported
findings.recommendation: Validate that the provided image extension is in an allowed list of valid image formats before processing

findings.description: Esecuzione di comandi shell al livello del moduli. Può bloccare l'avvio dell'applicazione o bloccare insallazione in ambienti non autorizzati.
findings.recommendation: Usa un installer specifico per DIPENDENZE o valida la presenza della dipendenza nell'ambiente di produzione prima dell'import.

findings.description: Esecuzione dinamica di comandi Python al momento dell'import, che introduce instabilità e potenziali rischi di sicurezza nel file system.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere l'esecuzione di script setup. Le dipendenze devono essere gestite tramite package manager o gestite globalmente.

findings.description: Uso di forzatura (wget) per scaricare pesi modelli tramite shell, espone al rischio di download da URL non sicuri o burst.
