    description="Access Key ID for authenticating with Baidu OBS",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Sposta le credenziali nel file .env e caricalo in runtime using dotenv library o pydantic_settings con include_file=True

findings.description: Secret Access key esposto in configurazione Python. Le chiavi segrete non devono mai essere in codice sorgente.
findings.code_snippet:
BAIDU_OBS_SECRET_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="Secret Access Key for authenticating with Baidu OBS",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Definisci un file .env con la posizione delle credenziali e caricalo usando os.getenv() o pydantic_settings con path_env solution

findings.description: Manca validazione che i campi sensibili non siano nulli in runtime, cosa che potrebbe causare fallimenti con message 'Credentiali OBS non configurate'.
findings.recommendation: Aggiungi Field(description="Must not be None", default=".env", **kwargs) per BAIDU_OBS_BUCKET_NAME e camposensibili,

findings.description: Mancanza di tipizzazione esplicita con validate-aware, il tipo str | None è appena supporto Python 3.10+
findings.recommendation: Considerare TypedDict o typing.ServiceAwares per compatibilità con versioni Python precedenti

findings.description: Manca configurazione per connessioni persistenti e pool, che potrebbe causare performance degradation在高并发scenario
findings.recommendation: Considera di aggiungere max_connections, retry_strategy, timeout per connessioni HTTP/OBS

findings.description: Manca documentazione inline per funzioni configurazionali e class docstring non spiegano il contesto d'uso
findings.code_snippet: """Configuration settings for Baidu Object Storage Service (OBS)"""
findings.recommendation: Amplifica docstring con esempi di uso, percorso consigliato, e limitazioni note

findings.description: REDIS_SSL_CERT_REQS predefinito su 'CERT_NONE', che disabilita la verifica dei certificati SSL. In ambienti di produzione, ciò espone il sistema a attacchi Man-in-the-Middle.
findings.recommendation: Modificare il default in 'CERT_OPTIONAL' o 'CERT_REQUIRED' per garantire che le connessioni Redis siano crittografate e verificate.

findings.description: REDIS_USE_SSL è impostato su False per default. Le connessioni Redis in rete devono essere cifrate di default per garantire la sicurezza dei dati sensibili.
findings.recommendation: Imbastire il default a True per forzare le connessioni sicure, permettendo lo switch solo tramite variabile d'ambiente in modalità sandbox.

findings.description: REDIS_USE_SENTINEL_SOCKET_TIMEOUT è impostato su 0.1 (100ms). Questo timeout è eccessivamente breve per la latenza di rete tipica e può causare falsi fallimenti delle connessioni.
findings.recommendation: Aumentare il valore predefinito a 2.0 o 5.0 secondi per garantire la stabilità della comunicazione con i nodi Sentinel.

findings.description: Mancanza di parametri per l'endpoint Redis Timeout (connection timeout). Le connessioni persistenti potrebbero bloccarsi se il server Redis non risponde.
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere un campo REDIS_SOCKET_TIMEOUT e REDIS_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT per gestire i timeout dei socket e le attese di connessione.

findings.description: Mancanza di docstring specifiche per i metodi o funzioni se questa classe fosse usata per istanziazione. È già ben descritta a livello di classe, ma i metodi potrebbero migliorare.
findings.code_snippet: // Tutto il file è config, useranno il BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Assicurarsi che la classe RedisConfig abbia implementazioni di metodi di inizializzazione adattabili con docstring dettagliate.

findings.description: Configuration field uses raw string type but base64-encoded JSON should be validated as JSON structure when loaded
findings.code_snippet: GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64: str | None = Field(description="Base64-encoded JSON key file for Google Cloud service account authentication", default=None,)
findings.recommendation: Add type validation to ensure the base64 string decodes to valid JSON when loaded from environment

findings.description: Configure field validation for edge cases (empty strings, invalid base64)
findings.code_snippet: GOOGLE_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(description="Name of the Google Cloud Storage bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'my-gcs-bucket')", default=None,)

findings.description: Service account JSON credentials configuration should have stricter documentation about loading from .env file, not passing directly
findings.code_snippet: GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64: str | None = Field(description="Base64-encoded JSON key file for Google Cloud service account authentication", default=None,)

findings.description: No explicit indication of how this config will be loaded - ensure it's loaded via proper settings initialization mechanism
findings.recommendation: Document how BaseSettings will be instantiated and loaded from environment variables

findings.description: Bucket name validation is missing - should ensure valid bucket name format
findings.code_snippet: GOOGLE_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(description="Name of the Google Cloud Storage bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'my-gcs-bucket')", default=None,)
findings.recommendation: Add Field validation with regex for Google Cloud Storage bucket naming conventions (a-z, 0-9, -, _. max 63 chars)

findings.description: Field name GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64 may encourage storing credentials in code. Service account JSON files should NEVER be committed to version control even when base64 encoded.
findings.recommendation: Document this field clearly that it must be loaded from environment variables or .env file only. Add configuration documentation to architecture folder.

findings.description: No validation on GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64 field. Invalid JSON could cause authentication failures or security issues during service account initialization.
findings.recommendation: Add JSON schema validation for the service account field. Verify base64 format and that decoded JSON structure is valid before use.

findings.description: Field name contains mixed case with underscores. Consider consistent convention like GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_KEY_B64 for clarity.
findings.recommendation: Standardize naming convention across configuration fields. Use consistent uppercase_snake_case.

findings.description: Sensitive credentials (Access Key and Secret Key) are stored in configuration class without validation/encryption, should be masked from logs and properly validated
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_OBS_ACCESS_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="Access Key ID for authenticating with Huawei Cloud OBS",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add field validator to ensure these secrets are never logged, and use pydantic.secretstr or v1 secrets exposed via environment variables only

findings.description: Configuration class has sensitive fields without proper documentation/guidance on .env file requirements
findings.recommendation: Add class docstring to explicitly state that sensitive fields must be loaded from .env file, not code

findings.description: Variable naming for sensitive fields could be clearer - consider indicating these require .env or ENV variables
findings.recommendation: Use prefix convention like 'OBS_ACCESS_KEY_ID' and document that these must come from environment variables via .env file

findings.description: No proof validation or constraints on the configuration values - should validate not both access_key and secret_key when using authentication
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Add root_validator or field_validators to ensure HUAWEI_OBS_ACCESS_KEY and HUAWEI_OBS_SECRET_KEY are both provided when authentication is required

findings.description: Sensitive fields (ACCESS_KEY, SECRET_KEY) should enforce required validation at startup to prevent storing invalid configurations
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_OBS_ACCESS_KEY: str | None = Field(
        description="Access Key ID for authenticating with Huawei Cloud OBS",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Change default=None to default_factory=() or use Field(default_factory=lambda: os.getenv('...')) to ensure these values are always provided via environment variables

findings.description: Missing import for type construct hints should use Optional[str] for compatibility across Python versions or ensure Python 3.10+ runtime
findings.recommendation: Add '# type: ignore' annotation for type hints compatibility or specify minimum Python 3.10 in requirements

findings.description: Configuration field names don't explicitly indicate they are mandatory or optional - unclear validation state
findings.recommendation: Add model_validator() to provide custom validation that ensures at least SERVER, ACCESS_KEY, and SECRET_KEY are all provided when using the configuration

findings.description: Field descriptions are embedded directly in Field() calls rather than external configuration schema or schema file
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_OBS_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(
        description="Name of the Huawei Cloud OBS bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'my-obs-bucket')",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Consider moving verbose descriptions to external schema.json file for better maintainability and separation of concerns

findings.description: Credenziali OCI (keys segrete e access key) sono dichiarate direttamente nel codice invece che come variabili d'ambiente
findings.code_snippet:
OCI_ACCESS_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="Access key (also known as API key)...",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Mantenere le credenziali nei file .env usando variabili d'ambiente. Leggere OCI_ACCESS_KEY e OCI_SECRET_KEY dal file di configurazione environment al runtime, non in codice.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices #5: Sensitive values should NEVER be in code, but only in .env

findings.description: La key segreta (Secret key) è esposta nel codice sorgente, violating principios di sicurezza fondamentali
findings.code_snippet:
OCI_SECRET_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="Secret key associated with the access key...",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Utilizzare l' approccio predefinito di PydanticBaseSettings che legge automaticamente dalle variabili d'ambiente .env. Rimuovere queste proprietà o renderle opzionali come impostazione.
findings.reference: Configuration Management #2: NO hardcoded parameters in code, Best Practice #5: Security

findings.description: Mancanza di validazione esplicita per gli endpoint e region OCI
findings.code_snippet:
OCI_ENDPOINT: str | None = Field(
    description="URL of the OCI Object Storage endpoint...",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere validator Pydantic per verificare l'URL e il schema dell'endpoint OCI (deve iniziare con https://)
findings.reference: Security Best Practices #5: Validate and sanitize all user inputs

findings.description: I default values sono None - considerare di forzare valori obbligatori per configuration
findings.recommendation: Considerare se questi parametri di configurazione devono obbligatoriamente essere forniti all'avvio
findings.reference: Configuration Management #2: Configuration should be loaded at startup

findings.description: OCI Access Key and Secret Key are defined in configuration class with descriptions that may leak sensitive information to developers reviewing the code
findings.code_snippet:
OCI_ACCESS_KEY: str | None = Field(
        description="Access key (also known as API key) for authenticating with OCI Object Storage",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Remove detailed descriptions of sensitive fields. Use minimal descriptions in source code and rely on documentation for security-sensitive information

findings.description: OCI Secret Key configuration includes description text that could expose credential patterns to unauthorized users viewing code
findings.code_snippet:
OCI_SECRET_KEY: str | None = Field(
        description="Secret key associated with the access key for authenticating with OCI Object Storage",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Reduce description restriction - only keep generic field type information (e.g., 'Oblivious secret key token') to minimize credential exposure through code inspection

findings.description: Valore di default hardcoded per l'endpoint del servizio di storage.
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere il default hardcoded.richiami questo valore tramite variabile d'ambiente (es. CLICKZETTA_VOLUME_SERVICE_URL) per permettere la configurazone in fase di deployment senza modificare il codice sorgente.

findings.description: Valori hardcoded per identittatori di infrastruttura (Workspace, VCluster).
findings.recommendation: Trasferire la definizione del nome del workspace e del vcluster in variabili d'ambiente per garantire l'indipendenza del software dall'ambiente specifico.

findings.description: Configurazione inflessibile su schema predefinito.
findings.recommendation: Permettere la configurazione dello schema tramite variabile d'ambiente. Configurare con un valore predefinito richiede modifiche nel codice o nel file di config al cambio di schema.

findings.description: Campi sensibili permettono valori nulli senza validazione globale obbligatoria.
findings.recommenation: Usare `min_length=1` o `Field(..., required=True)` accanto a `default_factory=lambda: os.getenv(...)` se i dati sono obbligatori per garantire che le credenziali siano sempre fornite.

findings.description: Schemi e workspace hardcoded possono portare a contesti di runtime non sicuri.
findings.recommendation: Evitare hardcoding di nomi logici come 'dify' per evitare configurazioni errate se la struttura logica cambia.

findings.description: Hardcoded default value without environment variable fallback configuration specified
findings.recommendation: Use construct=Frozen or add Field with env_prefix='OPENDAL่อย่าง minh để map với biến môi trường

findings.description: Field description could be more detailed
findings.recommendation: Add more context about the scheme types accepted (e.g., fs, memory, api, etc.)

findings.description: No validation constraints (min_length, max_length, pattern) for the scheme value
findings.recommendation: Add Field validators to ensure only valid schemes are accepted

findings.description: Hardcoded default value 'fs' for OPENDAL_SCHEME violates best practice of NO hardcoded parameters in code. This value should be configurable via environment variables to support different environments.
findings.recommendation: Replace hardcoded default with None to force environment variable usage, or use os.environ.get() pattern. Example: OPENDAL_SCHEME: str = Field(default=os.environ.get("OPENDAL_SCHEME", "fs"))

findings.description: No validation schema specified for the scheme parameter. It should be restricted to valid OpenDAL schemes (fs, s3, azure, etc.) to prevent runtime errors.
findings.code_snippet:
OPENDAL_SCHEME: str = Field(
        default="fs",
        description="OpenDAL scheme.",
    )
findings.recommendation: Add Enum validation for scheme: from typing import Literal; scheme: Literal['fs', 's3', 'azure', 'oss', 'gcs', 'alibaba', 'gif'] to ensure only valid schemes are accepted.

findings.description: Configuration field defines sensitive API key but has no secret validation or parsing hint - should use sensitive field markers to prevent accidental logging or exposure
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_API_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="API KEY for authenticating with Supabase",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add sensitive data marker: SUPABASE_API_KEY: str | None = Field(description=..., default=None, sensitive=True) or use con outlined secrets handler

findings.description: Using `str | None` union syntax requires Python 3.10+ - consider using Optional[str] for Python 3.9+ compatibility if needed
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Name of the Supabase bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'dify-bucket')",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Replace `str | None` with `Optional[str]` for better Python version compatibility

findings.description: Field descriptions could include examples directly in code comment or type hint for better discoverability
findings.code_snippet: description="Name of the Supabase bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'dify-bucket')"
findings.recommendation: Consider adding typed hint like: Field(description=..., examples=[...]) or separate config documentation

findings.description: Missing validation constraints on string fields - consider adding min_length constraints to prevent empty values
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_URL: str | None = Field(
    description="URL of the Supabase",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add validation: Field(default=None, min_length=10, description=...)

findings.description: Fields should have validation constraints to ensure required parameters are provided (e.g., SUPABASE_API_KEY should require validation)
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_API_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="API KEY for authenticating with Supabase",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add Field(default=..., validate=True) or use Field(..., description="...",) to make critical fields required, or implement validation after initialization to catch None values for sensitive fields

findings.description: Consider adding simulated min-length validation for SUPABASE_BUCKET_NAME to prevent invalid bucket names as they may violate Supabase naming conventions
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Name of the Supabase bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'dify-bucket')",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add pydantic Field validation: SUPABASE_BUCKET_NAME = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255) to ensure bucket name constraints

findings.description: Configuration loading should be single-loaded at application startup rather than dynamically loaded on-demand
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Ensure SupabaseStorageConfig is instantiated once at application startup and cached, not re-instantiated on each call to avoid redundant environment variable parsing

findings.description: Document that these fields must be configured in the .env file for the application to function
findings.recommendation: Add documentation comment or docstring mentioning required environment variables for deployment clarity

findings.description: Sensitive credentials (service account JSON) should not be hardcoded. All credentials must come from environment variables or .env file at runtime.
findings.code_snippet:
GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64: str | None = Field(
    description="Base64-encoded JSON key file for Google Cloud service account authentication",
    default=None,
  )
findings.recommendation: Load this value from environment variable (e.g., os.getenv('GOOGLE_STORAGE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON_BASE64', None)) instead of using Field with default. Reference: Best Practice #5 - Security Best Practices

findings.description: Variable names are too verbose. Prefix remove repetition (GOOGLE_STORAGE_ is used multiple times).
findings.recommendation: Use more concise naming like 'GCS_BUCKET_NAME' or 'GCS_NAME'. Best practice is readable config names without redundant prefixes. Reference: Best Practice #6 - Code Quality

findings.description: Missing input validation for configuration values. No length limits or format validation on bucket name or credentials.
findings.recommendation: Add Pydantic validators to ensure bucket names follow GCS naming rules (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores only, 3-63 chars). Reference: Best Practice #4 - Error Handling & Best Practice #5 - Security Best Practices

findings.description: Config class could be instantiated at module level, but consider lazy loading for large files like base64 JSON to save memory.
findings.recommendation: Consider lazy initialization of the credentials field if it's a large base64 string (can be 50K+ chars). Reference: Best Practice #3 - Performance Anti-Patterns

findings.description: La classe di configurazione esposta API_KEY di Supabase in modo diretto. Anche se `pydantic-settings` dovrebbe caricare dai file .env, non ci è indicata esplicita la configurazione per evitare hardcoded credentials
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_API_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="API KEY for authenticating with Supabase",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Assicurarsi che queste variabili si leggano esclusivamente da file .env tramite pydantic-settings e non siano mai hardcoded nel codice sorgente

findings.description: Manca il type e validation validation per gli campi sensibili. Supabase URL e API_KEY dovrebbero avere pattern validation o lunghezza minima
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Name of the Supabase bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'dify-bucket')",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere Field validators per validazione: Field(min_length=1) o regex per validare bucket names convengono

findings.description: La classe non validate se le credentials siano state correttamente caricate da .env prima dell'uso. Potrebbe permettere referenze incomplete
findings.code_snippet:
SUPABASE_URL: str | None = Field(
        description="URL of the Supabase",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere post-init validation che control that i variables are properly set when used to load from environment variables

findings.description: Credenziali di autenticazione (SECRET_ID, SECRET_KEY) sono esposte come campi di configurazione senza validazione che vengano caricate da variabili d'ambiente o file .env
findings.code_snippet:
TENCENT_COS_SECRET_ID: str | None = Field(
    description="SecretId for authenticating with Tencent Cloud COS (part of API credentials)",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Configura BaseSettings per caricare automaticamente questi valori dalle variabili d'ambiente (TENCENT_COS_SECRET_ID, TENCENT_COS_SECRET_KEY) e aggiungi validazione per garantire che non siano None in produzione

findings.description: Nessuna validazione di sicurezza per parametri sensibili - i valori possono rimanere None causando fallimenti non ergonomici in runtime
findings.code_snippet:
TENCENT_COS_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Name of the Tencent Cloud COS bucket to store and retrieve objects",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Aggiungi metodi di validazione post-inizializzazione per verificare che i campi obbligatori (BUCkET_NAME, REGION) siano stati forniti, altrimenti fallisci con errore chiaro all'avvio

findings.description: L'uso di (str | None) richiede Python 3.10+. Per maggiore compatibilità, considera l'uso delle otto da```typing.py] import Optional
findings.code_snippet:
TENCENT_COS_BUCKET_NAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Name of the Tencent Cloud COS bucket to store and retrieve objects",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Importa Optional da typing e usa Optional[str] per migliore compatibilità con Python versioni precedenti a 3.10

findings.description: Configurazioni non documentate - manchi di commenti che spieghino / quali sono i valori validi per ogni campo
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Aggiungi docstring al campo classe che elenchi valori validi (es. schema per TENCENT_COS_SCHEME deve essere 'https' in produzione)

findings.description: Sensitive fields (SECRET_ID, SECRET_KEY) have None as default which could cause authentication failures at runtime
findings.recommendation: Change default from None to empty string ('') or make them required fields. Better yet, validate that these are loaded from environment variables at runtime using pydantic_settings automagic.

findings.description: No environment variable validation for sensitive configuration values
findings.recommendation: Add Field(validation_alias='TENCENT_COS_SECRET_ID') to show explicit env var mapping, or add model_post_init() validation to reject configs with missing credentials.

findings.description: No documentation or comments about required environment variables for this configuration
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Add a README or docstring in the module documentation listing required environment variables (TENCENT_COS_BUCKET_NAME, TENCENT_COS_REGION, etc.) and their minimum safe values.

findings.description: PEP 8 naming convention violation: variable names should use underscores for environments but current naming is consistent with config pattern
findings.recommendation: While PEP 8 prefers underscores, this follows the config pattern correctly. However consider adding MODEL_VALIDATIONS or alias='TENCENT_COS_BUCKET_NAME' for explicit env var mapping.

findings.description: API credentials (SECRET_ID and SECRET_KEY) stored in a module-level config class. If this file is committed to version control, credentials could be exposed and compromise cloud resource access.
findings.code_snippet:
TENCENT_COS_SECRET_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="SecretKey for authenticating with Tencent Cloud COS (part of API credentials)",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Ensure sensitive values are NEVER in configuration source files. Only load from environment variables using external .env files that are added to .gitignore.

findings.description: Source credentials stored in code could leak through version control, CI/CD pipelines, or accidental file sharing. API secrets should only be loaded from secure environment variables at runtime.
findings.recommendation: Add BaseSettings validation with pydantic.env_settings and ensure this file is excluded from source control. Use .env files with .gitignore protection.

findings.description: TENCENT_COS_SCHEME defaults to None allowing potential use of http instead of https, which exposes data in transit to interception risks.
findings.code_snippet:
TENCENT_COS_SCHEME: str | None = Field(
    description="Protocol scheme for COS requests: 'https' (recommended) or 'http'",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Default SCHEME to 'https' and require https for all external communications as per security best practices.

findings.description: No validation constraints on sensitive credential keys - empty or invalid values could lead to authentication failures or insecure default behaviors.
findings.recommendation: Add Field validators (Field(...) with validators) to validate credential format, length, and non-empty status when values are provided.

findings.description: Detailed description strings in Field may leak sensitive information hints about exposed credentials structure.
findings.code_snippet:
TENCENT_COS_SECRET_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="SecretKey for authenticating with Tencent Cloud COS (part of API credentials)",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Reduce Field descriptions to minimal required context. Detailed credential structure information should not be in source code.

findings.description: Credenziali sensibili (ACCESS_KEY, SECRET_KEY) sono visibili nel file di configurazione invece di essere obbligatoriamente impostabili solo tramite .env file tramite variables d'ambiente
findings.recommendation: Rendere i campi di credenziali come required e documentare che devono essere impostati esclusivamente tramite vari d'ambiente o file .env

findings.description: Nessuna validazione sui campi configurati - se impostati come None, gli errori si verificheranno solo in runtime
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere Field(valid=True) o ValidatedValidator per richiedere validazione sui campi critici (endpoint, access_key non possono essere None)

findings.description: Nomi delle variabili non seguono convenzioni standard: 'Volcengine' dovrebbe essere 'Volcengine' (corretto) ma 'VOLCENGINE_TOS' potrebbe usare underscore case standard
findings.recommendation: Standardizzare naming convention: utilizzare snake_case per le configurazioni, verificare la consistentza tra access_key_id e access_key

findings.description: Manca input validation per URL dell'endpoint - non viene verificata se è una URL valida
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere validation pattern per l'URL endpoint usando pydantic's field_validator

findings.description: In the description field for HUAWEI_OBS_SERVER (Line 26), there is a hardcoded example URL for Huawei Cloud OBS. If this file is part of public documentation or exposed code, this infrastructure detail could be disclosed.
findings.code_snippet: description="Endpoint URL for Huawei Cloud OBS (e.g., 'https://obs.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com')"
findings.recommendation: Remove specific internal infrastructure examples from comments and docstrings. Use generic placeholders like 'YOUR_ENDPOINT_HERE'.
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Sensitive Data Exposure, Code Quality - Magic Numbers (constants)

findings.description: Password has hardcoded default empty string which could allow insecure configurations and doesn't enforce .env file usage
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_PASSWORD: str = Field(
        description="Password for authenticating with AlibabaCloud MySQL (default is an empty string)",
        default="",
    )
findings.recommendation: Remove default value for password field; require it to be set via .env file or at runtime. Add validation to reject empty password.

findings.description: Database name is hardcoded with project-specific reference 'dify', violates task-agnostic principle
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_DATABASE: str = Field(
        description="Name of the AlibabaCloud MySQL database to connect to (default is 'dify')",
        default="dify",
    )
findings.recommendation: Remove default database name configuration. Let it be configurable via runtime parameters or environment variables.

findings.description: Username field has hardcoded default 'root' which is a security anti-pattern for production databases
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_USER: str = Field(
        description="Username for authenticating with AlibabaCloud MySQL (default is 'root')",
        default="root",
    )
findings.recommendation: Remove default username. Enforce configuration via environment variables or external config.

findings.description: HNSW_M field has range description (3-200) but no actual validation enforcement in the Field definition
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_HNSW_M: PositiveInt = Field(
        description="Maximum number of connections per layer for HNSW vector index (default is 6, range: 3-200)",
        default=6,
    )
findings.recommendation: Add validation using Field(..., ge=3, le=200) to enforce the stated range limits at runtime.

findings.description: No import validation or runtime checks exist for duplicate config loading if this module is imported multiple times
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field, PositiveInt
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a singleton pattern or module-level constant to ensure only one config instance is active.

findings.description: Field description 'my-tos-bucket' shows example bucket name which could be considered as exposing test configuration details in source code
findings.code_snippet: description="Name of the Volcengine TOS bucket to store and retrieve objects (e.g., 'my-tos-bucket')"
findings.recommendation: Remove example values from field descriptions in sensitive configuration files

findings.description: API credentials are stored in a configuration class without proper secret field validation, making them potentially accessible if source code is exposed
findings.code_snippet: VOLCENGINE_TOS_SECRET_KEY: str | None = Field(..., description="Secret Access Key for authenticating with Volcengine TOS", default=None)
findings.recommendation: Use pydantic.SecretField or ensure all credentials are loaded exclusively from .env files with proper validation

findings.description: No Required field flag set - if optional fields are used, may result in missing authentication or authorization
findings.code_snippet: VOLCENGINE_TOS_ACCESS_KEY: str | None = Field(..., description="Access Key ID for authenticating with Volcengine TOS", default=None)
findings.recommendation: Mark critical authentication fields as Required() with max_length and min_length constraints for proper validation

findings.description: Endpoint example 'https://tos-cn-beijing.volces.com' and region 'cn-beijing' are documented in source code, could leak internal infrastructure info
findings.code_snippet: description="URL of the Volcengine TOS endpoint (e.g., 'https://tos-cn-beijing.volces.com')"
findings.recommendation: Remove infrastructure examples from source code, use generic descriptions or separate documentation

findings.description: Password is hardcoded with default value in code. Credentials should NEVER be in code, must be loaded from .env file using environment variables
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_PASSWORD: str = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with AlibabaCloud MySQL (default is an empty string)",
    default="",
)
findings.recommendation: Move password to .env file and load using pydantic_settings from BASE_DIR/env file: MYSQL_PASSWORD = os.getenv('ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_PASSWORD')
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Sensitive values (API keys, passwords) should NEVER be in code, but only in file .env

findings.description: Username defaults to 'root' which is a default administrative account. Hardcoded database credentials in code pose security risks
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_USER: str = Field(
    description="Username for authenticating with AlibabaCloud MySQL (default is 'root')",
    default="root",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove default username and require it to be set via environment variable. Never use root as default password or username in production

findings.description: Project-specific database name 'dify' hardcoded in config file. This reduces code agnosticism and leaks project context into configuration
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_DATABASE: str = Field(
    description="Name of the AlibabaCloud MySQL database to connect to (default is 'dify')",
    default="dify",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove the default database name from code. Use environment variable and allow the application to use the database specified in deployment configuration
findings.reference: Code Agnosticism - Avoid hardcoding specific task details

findings.description: Configuration defaults are set in code instead of from environment variables. All sensitive and environment-specific configuration should be loaded at startup from .env or config files
findings.recommendation: Initialize all values as required or empty with loading logic at startup: config = AlibabaCloudMySQLConfig.from_dotenv()
findings.reference: Configuration Management - All constant values should be in settings files, use environment variables for environment-specific configuration

findings.description: HNSW_M parameter has range mentioned but no validation constraints are enforced. Consider using validator to ensure M stays within 3-200 range
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_HNSW_M: PositiveInt = Field(
    description="Maximum number of connections per layer for HNSW vector index (default is 6, range: 3-200)",
    default=6,
)
findings.recommendation:
Add a validator to enforce range constraints: @field_validator('ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_HNSW_M')
    def validate_hnsw_m(cls, v): 
        assert 3 <= v <= 200, "HNSW_M must be between 3 and 200"
        return v
findings.reference: Code Quality - Use type hints and validation where applicable

findings.description: No explicit settings validation for password. Consider requiring password to be non-empty for security compliance in production
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_PASSWORD: str = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with AlibabaCloud MySQL (default is an empty string)",
    default="",
)
findings.reference: Security Best Practices - Validate and sanitize all user inputs

findings.description: Default username 'root' is hardcoded in source code, exposing default authentication credentials
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_USER: str = Field(
        description="Username for authenticating with AlibabaCloud MySQL (default is 'root')",
        default="root",
    )
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded default username. Require users to provide their own credentials via .env file or external configuration at runtime

findings.description: Database name 'dify' is hardcoded as default, potentially exposing application-specific database information
findings.code_snippet:
ALIBABACLOUD_MYSQL_DATABASE: str = Field(
        description="Name of the AlibabaCloud MySQL database to connect to (default is 'dify')",
        default="dify",
    )
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded database name. Users should configure this value based on their deployment environment

findings.description: Password field has a default value that includes empty string in comments, risking password-related hardcoded configuration
findings.recommendation: Remove all password-related default values from code. Passwords should never be mentioned or defaulted in source code

findings.description: No input validation or sanitization on connection parameters before use in database operations
findings.recommendation: Add validation logic to verify config values are properly sanitized and meet security requirements before database connection

findings.description: Sensitive configuration field naming uses all uppercase which may inadvertently expose sensitive values in error logs
findings.recommendation: Consider using underscore_case naming convention for configuration to reduce risk of exposure in logs or error messages

findings.description: Line comments indicate hardcoded credential field names but actual values should be loaded from environment variables (.env file) - ensure _CONF values are NOT in the code itself
findings.code_snippet:
ANALYTICDB_KEY_ID: str | None = Field(
        default=None, description="The Access Key ID provided by Alibaba Cloud for API authentication."
findings.recommendation: Ensure actual credential values are loaded from the environment (os.environ or pydantic-settings environment values) and NOT from default hardcoded values in this file.

findings.description: Lines 50-51: Description string is awkwardly split with newline in the middle affecting readability
findings.code_snippet: " (usually the initial account created with the instance).",
findings.recommendiation: Break long string descriptions across multiple lines using implicit string concatenation rather than mid-description newline placement.

findings.description: Consider adding validation for required sensitive parameters - fields should have minimum_length validation for password fields
findings.code_snippet:
ANALYTICDB_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
        default=None, description="The password associated with the AnalyticDB account for database authentication."
findings.recommendation: Add Field(default=None, min_length=8, ...) to ensure password field has minimum length validation if values are provided.

findings.description: Hardcoded default port value (5432) could be more configurable via environment
findings.code_snippet:
ANALYTICDB_PORT: PositiveInt = Field(
        default=5432, description="The port of the AnalyticDB instance you want to connect to."
findings.recommendation: Allow port to be configurable via environment variable if needed for flexibility across different deployment environments.

findings.description: L'uso della sintassi `str | None` richiede Python 3.10+. Per massizzare la compatibilità e seguire mejores pratiche di tipizzazione.
findings.recommendation: Importare `from typing import Optional` e usare `Optional[str]` per la compatibilità con versioni precedenti di Python.

findings.description: Manca la configurazione esplicita del file `.env` per il caricamento delle impostazioni di sicurezza.
findings.recommendation: Specificare la fonte dei file di configurazione tramite `model_config` o impostazioni opzionali `pyproject.toml` per centralizzare le fonti di configurazione.

findings.description: Il campo `Field` prende `default=None`, ma non è consigliato omettere valori di default per configurazioni sensibili se si vogliono evitare valori in produzione.
findings.recommendation: Usare `Field(description='...', env='OCI_ACCESS_KEY')` invece di `default=None` per rendere obbligatorio tramite variabili d'ambiente.

findings.description: API key configuration should not be hardcoded in source code. Sensitive values like BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_API_KEY must be loaded from environment variables or .env files.
findings.code_snippet:
BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_API_KEY: str | None = Field(
        description="API key for authenticating with the Baidu Vector Database service",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Migrate the API key to environment variables or .env file. Use os.getenv('BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_API_KEY') or pydantic.config.load_dotenv() to load from external files.

findings.description: Account credentials should be stored in environment variables, not in source code files. This prevents credential exposure in version control.
findings.code_snippet:
BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_ACCOUNT: str | None = Field(
        description="Account for authenticating with the Baidu Vector Database",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Store BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_ACCOUNT in .env file and load it at application startup using environment variables.

findings.description: Hardcoded default values should be moved to configuration files. The default values 30000, 1, 3, DEFAULT_ANALYZER, COARSE_MODE are magic constants that reduce clarity and maintainability.
findings.code_snippet:
BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MS: PositiveInt = Field(
        description="Timeout in milliseconds for Baidu Vector Database operations (default is 30000 milliseconds)",
        default=30000,
    )
findings.recommendation: Move default values to a dedicated configuration file (e.g., configs/vdb_defaults.py) or use Pydantic defaults in a base class for centralized management.

findings.description: Missing type hints on non-nullable fields. Using str | None without default also means type hints are incomplete - add typing import if needed.
findings.code_snippet: from pydantic import Field, NonNegativeInt, PositiveInt
findings.recommendation: Add Literal or typing imports if needed. Consider using typing.Self or proper Optional hints with Optional[str] syntax for consistency.

findings.description: Configuration description mentions hardcoded values in comments which may become outdated. Default values mentioned in descriptions should match actual implementation.
findings.code_snippet:
description="Timeout in milliseconds for Baidu Vector Database operations (default is 30000 milliseconds)",
        default=30000,
findings.recommendation: Standardize all default value mentions in docstrings with the actual code. Consider using a separate defaults module to keep descriptions and values in sync.

findings.description: API key field allows default=None but shows API key configuration is in code. API keys should only be loaded from environment variables (.env file) never hardcoded or exposed in source code.
findings.code_snippet:
BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_API_KEY: str | None = Field(
    description="API key for authenticating with the Baidu Vector Database service",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Set this field to load from environment variable using: from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings; class BaiduVectorDBConfig(BaseSettings): BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_API_KEY: str = Field(default=import os).getenv('BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_API_KEY')

findings.description: Account and API key fields are configuration fields for authentication. These should be loaded from .env file rather than having defaults defined in source code which may be committed to version control.
findings.code_snippet:
BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_ACCOUNT: str | None = Field(
    description="Account for authenticating with the Baidu Vector Database",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Configure default to load from environment variable with os.getenv('BAIDU_VECTOR_DB_ACCOUNT') instead of None. Never store credentials in commit.

findings.description: Endpoint URL example contains hardcoded URL 'http://vdb.bj.baidubce.com' in description string at line 14. While just documentation, avoid URL examples that could reveal infrastructure details.
findings.code_snippet: description="URL of the Baidu Vector Database service (e.g., 'http://vdb.bj.baidubce.com')"
findings.recommendation: Use generic placeholder in docstring like (e.g., '<protocol>://<endpoint>') or remove example to avoid exposing infrastructure information.

findings.description: Hardcoded default port (8000) should be configurable via environment variables instead of being directly in the code
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_PORT: PositiveInt = Field(
    description="Port number on which the Chroma server is listening (default is 8000)",
    default=8000,
  )
findings.recommendation: Change default to None and load from environment variable using pydantic-settings settings_next)

findings.description: CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS field stores sensitive data (passwords/tokens) with None default, not properly loading from secure .env file
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS: str | None = Field(
    description="Authentication credentials for Chroma (format depends on the auth provider)",
    default=None,
  )
findings.recommendation: Use pydantic's env_file import and ensure sensitive values are loaded from .env file with proper masking/logging

findings.description: Field names use conventional ALL_CAPS naming but don't clearly indicate they're environment variable mappings (would be clearer with underscore prefix like CHROMA__HOST or similar convention)
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_HOST: str | None = Field(
    description="Hostname or IP address of the Chroma server (e.g., 'localhost' or '192.168.1.100')",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add descriptive prefix or use environment variable prefix syntax (e.g., DB_CHROMA_HOST) for clarity on where config comes from

findings.description: CHROMA_* prefix is used but creates potential for future naming conflicts with other Chroma configs or modules
findings.recommendation: Consider using a config prefix parameter or cleaner namespace (e.g., ChromaConfig class with descriptive field names like 'host' not 'CHROMA_HOST')

findings.description: CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS stores sensitive authentication credentials that should not be hardcoded, even in configuration classes.
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS: str | None = Field(
        description="Authentication credentials for Chroma (format depends on the auth provider)",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Migrate credentials to environment variables only. Use Field(...=os.getenv('CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS')) or Pydantic's env_fields_config to force loading from environment variables.

findings.description: Configuration defaults do not reference environment variables, which can lead to inconsistent configuration loading.
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_PORT: PositiveInt = Field(
        description="Port number on which the Chroma server is listening (default is 8000)",
        default=8000,
    )
findings.recommendation: Update all config fields to reference environment variables using pydantic field() with env_prefix or use Pydantic v2's env_settings pattern.

findings.description: Field defaults rely on hardcoded magic numbers (8000) rather than environment variables or external config.
findings.recommendation: Allow Port to be overridden via environment variable to support different deployment environments.

findings.description: Use of str | None union syntax requires Python 3.10+, which may cause compatibility issues with older runtime versions.
findings.recommendation: Consider using Optional[str] for Python 3.9+ compatibility or set minimum Python version in requirements.

findings.description: Gli attributi della classe utilizzano convenzione UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (es. VOLCENGINE_TOS_BUCKET_NAME), mentre PEP 8 raccomanda snake_case per le variabili e gli attributi di classe (snake_case).
findings.recommendation: Riformattare gli attributi in snake_case (es. tos_bucket_name) per garantire coerenza con le convenzioni PEP 8 per le variabili Python.

findings.description: Assenza di validazione personalizzata per verificare la presenza di credenziali prima dell'uso. Se VOLCENGINE_TOS_ACCESS_KEY è None, l'applicazione potrebbe fallire runtime senza un avvertimento esplicito.
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere un validator Pydantic per garantire che chiavi fondamentali (Key, Secret) non siano nulli se l'endpoint è configurato.

findings.description: L'uso di descrizione nell'attributo Field è valido, ma la docstring di classe non specifica le dipendenze critiche o fallback.
findings.code_snippet:
"""
    Configuration settings for Volcengine Torch Object Storage (TOS)
    """
findings.recommendation: Espandere la docstring per includere avvertenze sulla sicurezza (es. 'Le credenziali devono essere fornite via environment variables').

findings.description: Credenziali di autenticazione potrebbero essere hardcodeate se CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS fornisce default value non None o viene impostata direttamente nel codice invece che da variabili d'ambiente
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS: str | None = Field(
    description="Authentication credentials for Chroma (format depends on the auth provider)",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Assicurati che CHROMA_AUTH_CREDENTIALS venga sempre impostato tramite variabili d'ambiente (.env file) e mai direttamente nel codice

findings.description: Porta hardcoded con un numero (8000) come value predefinita invece che essere configurabile esternamente
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_PORT: PositiveInt = Field(
    description="Port number on which the Chroma server is listening (default is 8000)",
    default=8000,
)
findings.recommendation: Mappleare CHROMA_PORT come variabile d'ambiente con un valore predefinito di fallback

findings.description: Mancanza di validation regolata per hostname, database name, tenant identifier che potrebbero contenere caratteri pericolosi
findings.code_snippet:
CHROMA_HOST: str | None = Field(
    description="Hostname or IP address of the Chroma server (e.g., 'localhost' or '192.168.1.100')",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere validatori Pydantic per validare i hostname (es. uid('/./.' data) regex per IP) e database/tenant names

findings.description: Hardcoded default values in configuration (line 27-34, 36-44) violate best practice #2. All constant values should be set via environment variables to prevent leakage in version control.
findings.recommendation: Remove all hardcoded defaults except truly common ones. Load sensitive values from .env file via pydantic_settings

findings.description: Password field exposed in configuration (line 18-23) - should NEVER be hardcoded. Use environment variables only for authentication credentials per best practice #5.
findings.code_snippet:
CLICKZETTA_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with Clickzetta Lakehouse",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Ensure this value is ONLY loaded from environment variable, never set a default. Mark as sensitive in code review

findings.description: Multiple magic numbers and string literals (line 18-42) - batch size "100", default workspace "default", cluster "default_ap", analyzer "chinese", mode "smart", distance "cosine_distance" should be configurable.
findings.recommendation: Use constant MODULES.constants or environment variables for all configurable values. At minimum, make batch_size configurable at runtime

findings.description: Field naming uses extended prefix for all fields (CLICKZETTA_BATCH_SIZE, CLICKZETTA_ENABLE_INVERTED_INDEX, etc.) - slightly verbose but consistent. Consider grouping settings by functionality.
findings.recommendation: Create sub-classes for different configuration categories (credentials, indexing, database) for better organization and maintainability

findings.description: Hardcoded magic numbers used as default values (e.g., 30000, 1, 3). These should be defined in a separate constants file or environment variables to avoid committing specific values to source control.
findings.recommendation: Move hardcoded constants to a separate constants module or use environment variable defaults.

findings.description: Internal infrastructure URL exposed in field description within docstring (vdb.bj.baidubce.com). This could compromise security posture if the file is mirrored publicly.
findings.code_snippet: description="URL of the Baidu Vector Database service (e.g., 'http://vdb.bj.baidubce.com')"
findings.recommendation: Remove specific internal service URLs from documentation descriptions.

findings.description: Use of `str | None` syntax requires Python 3.10+. Consider using `Optional[str]` from typing or `from __future__ import annotations` for broader Python version compatibility.
findings.recommendation: Use `Optional[str]` or ensure project-wide `annotations` import for the `|` union syntax.

findings.description: Sensitive fields (KEY_SECRET, PASSWORD, NS_PASSWORD) use 'str' type. It is recommended to use 'SecretStr' from pydantic for credential fields to enforce secure masking and handling during logging or debugging, per Best Practice #5 (Security Best Practices).
findings.recommendation: Import 'SecretStr' from 'pydantic' and change field types to 'SecretStr' to better protect credentials.

findings.description: Magic numbers are hardcoded as defaults (5432 port, 1/5 connections). While this file is a settings file (Best Practice #2), hardcoded connection parameters (Pool) should ideally be derived from environment variables or centralized dynamic configuration to support environment changes easily.
findings.recommendation: Move specific connection limits to environment variables or a separate configuration section, rather than hardcoding defaults in this configuration model.

findings.description: Description text for fields 'ANALYTICDB_RESOURCE' and 'ANALYTICDB_MIN_CONNECTION' has grammatical inconsistencies (e.g., 'Min connection of the AnalyticDB database' lacks articles).
findings.code_snippet: ANALYTICDB_MIN_CONNECTION: PositiveInt = Field(default=1, description="Min connection of the AnalyticDB database.")
findings.recommendation: Refine descriptions to standard grammar (e.g., 'Minimum connection pool size for the AnalyticDB database.') for better documentation quality.

findings.description: El className debería incluir docstring/explicación del propósito específico de esta configuración
findings.recommendation: Añade exposición del docstring para explicar qué configuración específica contiene y cómo se utiliza

findings.description: Los nombres de variables están en mayúsculas completas siguiendo el patrón ENV vars, pero no es Pythonic naming convention
findings.recommendation: Considerar usar mayor heredabilidad: `couchbase_connection_string` para mayor legibilidad del código Pydantic

findings.description: Falta validación/documentación sobre qué variables son requeridas vs opcionales
findings.recommendation: Añadir `required=True` o `required=False` explícitamente en Field para clarificar el comportamiento

findings.description: Manca di validazione per campi obbligatori - se COUCHBASE_CONNECTION_STRING, COUCHBASE_USER o COUCHBASE_PASSWORD sono empty/null, l'applicazione potrebbe fallire in produzione con errore non chiaro
findings.code_snippet: COUCHBASE_CONNECTION_STRING: str | None = Field(description="COUCHBASE connection string", default=None,)
findings.recommendation: Cambiare default da None a empty string e aggiungere validation() method per required params, oppure rimuovere optional per parametri obbligati (except password)

findings.description: Assenza di validazione sui campi obbligatori. Assicurarsi che i parametri critici debbano essere sempre presenti
findings.code_snippet: COUCHBASE_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(description="COUCHBASE password", default=None,)
findings.recommendation: Impostare default='' o null con validator che controlli la presenza del password prima dell'uso: @validator('COUCHBASE_PASSWORD', always=True)

findings.description: Documentazione insufficiente. La classe ha docstring minimale ma non spiega quali sono i parametri obbligatori vs opzionali
findings.recommendation: Espandere docstring con lista di parametri obbligatori, opzionali, e valori di default. Aggiungere example di configurazione

findings.description: Mancante documentation di riferimento (best practice #8)
findings.recommendation: Creare file di documentazione in architecture/ folder che spieghi come questa config viene usata e quali parametri richiede

findings.description: Nessun caching delle configurazioni - tutte le configurazioni ricaricate con ogni nuova istanza
findings.recommendation: Considerare di cachingare l'istanza di configurazione al startup del servizio per evitare ricaricamento continuo

findings.description: Configuration class contains multiple sensitive fields (API keys, passwords) that should be validated to ensure they come from secure sources (environment variables) rather than being potentially hardcoded or logged
findings.recommendation: Ensure all sensitive fields (KEY_ID, KEY_SECRET, PASSWORD fields) are strictly validated to only accept environment variable inputs, and add /config/entrypoints to security logging policies to prevent sensitive data from being logged

findings.description: Configuration values could potentially be injected directly from code. Best practice is to use .env files or environment variables exclusively
findings.recommendation: Use pydantic-settings with strict validation to enforce environment variable loading for all credential fields, and add an __init__.py guard to ensure no direct instantiation without proper configuration

findings.description: ELASTICSEARCH_MAX_RETRIES is set to 10000, which is excessively high and could cause performance issues during connection attempts, especially in high-traffic scenarios or when testing environments
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_MAX_RETRIES: int = Field(
    description="Maximum number of retry attempts (default is 10000)", 
    default=10000
)
findings.recommendation: Reduce default to 100-500 retries maximum, with configurable separate max retries per connection

findings.description: ELASTICSEARCH_REQUEST_TIMEOUT of 100 seconds may cause connection pooling and request queueing issues in high-traffic scenarios
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_REQUEST_TIMEOUT: int = Field(
    description="Request timeout in milliseconds (default is 100000)", 
    default=100000
)
findings.recommendation: Set default timeout to 30000-50000ms (30-50 seconds) for better performance under load

findings.description: ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME and ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD have hardcoded defaults that could trigger unnecessary connection attempts during validation
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Username for authenticating with Elasticsearch...", 
    default="elastic"
)
findings.recommendation: Consider making defaults optional (None) and require explicit cloud/configuration to use them, reducing unnecessary auth attempts

findings.description: Hardcoded sensitive credentials (username and password) with insecure default values
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Username for authenticating with Elasticsearch (default is 'elastic')",
    default="elastic",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded defaults for username/password. Require these to be set via environment variables or .env file only

findings.description: Hardcoded sensitive credentials (username and password) with insecure default values
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with Elasticsearch (default is 'elastic')",
    default="elastic",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded defaults for username/password. Require these to be set via environment variables or .env file only

findings.description: Sensitive API key field allows None default which may lead to insecure connections
findings.code_snippet: ELASTICSEARCH_API_KEY: str | None = Field(description="API key for authenticating with Elastic Cloud",  default=None)
findings.recommendation: Ensure API key is always required when using cloud mode. Add validation in model_validator to enforce proper credential configuration

findings.description: SSL certificate verification disabled by default can expose to man-in-the-middle attacks
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_VERIFY_CERTS: bool = Field(
    description="Whether to verify SSL certificates (default is False)",
    default=False
  )
findings.recommendation: Change default to True for production security. Disable verification only for development environments with proper separate config

findings.description: Request timeout value of 100000ms (100 seconds) is excessively long
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_REQUEST_TIMEOUT: int = Field(
    description="Request timeout in milliseconds (default is 100000)",
    default=100000
  )
findings.recommendation: Reduce default timeout to 30000-10000ms range. 100 seconds timeout may cause connection leaks and resource exhaustion

findings.description: Max retries value of 10000 is excessively high and may cause DoS-like behavior
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_MAX_RETRIES: int = Field(
    description="Maximum number of retry attempts (default is 10000)",
    default=10000
  )
findings.recommendation: Reduce max retries to 3-5 attempts maximum. Set should correspond to request timeout to avoid excessive retry loops

findings.description: Magic numbers should be defined as named constants in a separate config file
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_PORT: PositiveInt = Field(
    description="Port number on which the Elasticsearch server is listening (default is 9200)",
    default=9200
  )
findings.recommendation: Create a constants file (elasticsearch_defaults.py) with standard port numbers and reference them to avoid magic numbers

findings.description: This configuration file does not use async/await operations. For future improvements, consider using async-capable settings loaders if the configuration is loaded frequently in async contexts.
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: No change needed for config files. If this class is instantiated frequently in async environments, ensure the loader uses async methods.

findings.description: Username and password defaults are hardcoded in code ('elastic') which exposes credentials if environment variables are not properly configured
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Username for authenticating with Elasticsearch (default is 'elastic')",
    default="elastic",
)
ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with Elasticsearch (default is 'elastic')",
    default="elastic",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove default credentials from code. Credentials should ALWAYS be loaded from environment variables (.env file). Set defaults to None or empty strings.

findings.description: Default username 'elastic' exposed in source code violates security best practices - credentials should never be visible in code
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME: str | None = Field(
    description="Username for authenticating with Elasticsearch (default is 'elastic')",
    default="elastic",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded username default. Use environment variable reference or set default to None and enforce presence in validation.

findings.description: Default password 'elastic' clearly visible in source code creates security risk and prevents proper secret management
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with Elasticsearch (default is 'elastic')",
    default="elastic",
)
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded password. Require ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD environment variable to be set in .env file. Validate that it's not empty after loading.

findings.description: SSL certificate verification is disabled by default (False), making the application vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_VERIFY_CERTS: bool = Field(
    description="Whether to verify SSL certificates (default is False)",
    default=False
)
findings.recommendation: Set default to True for production environments. Disable only in local development with clear warning. Verify certificates by default to prevent MITM attacks.

findings.description: Max retries set to 10000 is excessive and may indicate poor request handling, could cause system hangs or excessive resource consumption
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_MAX_RETRIES: int = Field(
    description="Maximum number of retry attempts (default is 10000)",
    default=10000
)
findings.recommendation: Reduce max retries to a reasonable value (5-10 is typical). Implement exponential backoff with jitter to prevent thundering herd and excessive resource consumption.

findings.description: Request timeout of 100000ms (100 seconds) is excessive and may leave connections open, blocking resources
findings.code_snippet:
ELASTICSEARCH_REQUEST_TIMEOUT: int = Field(
    description="Request timeout in milliseconds (default is 100000)",
    default=100000
)
findings.recommendation: Reduce timeout to 30000ms (30 seconds) as default. Longer timeouts should be configurable per task/operation, not global default.

findings.description: I modelli di configurazione non dovrebbero permettere valori hardcoded. Tutte le credenziali sensibili (HUAWEI_CLOUD_PASSWORD) devono essere fornITE SOLO tramite variabili d'ambiente o file .env, non come default in codice
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
        description="Password for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Rimuovere i valori predefiniti per i dati sensibili. Assicurarsi che il valore venga fornito esclusivamente tramite necessario environment variables (es. import passare dotenv al momento startup)

findings.description: Utilizzo di syntax tipizzazione `str | None` che richiede Python 3.10+. Per compatibilità, utilizzare `Optional[str]` da typing o `Union[str, None]`
findings.recommendation: Sostituire `str | None` con `Optional[str]` per garantire compatibilità con versioni Python più antiche

findings.description: Mancano regole di validazione per i parametri sensibili (es. lunghezza minima password,formato email per username). I dati dovrebbe essere sanificati e validati
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_USER: str | None = Field(
        description="Username for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere Field(content_validators) per validazione input. Es: password_min_length(), username_pattern()

findings.description: Manca nomato 'from typing import Optional' se si usa `Optional[str]` per evitare silent import errors
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere 'from typing import Optional' in case si us sole `Optional[str]` per maggiore explicit

findings.description: La descrizione delle credenziali è riduttiva. Considerare di aggiungere note su dove devono essere (solo .env)
findings.code_snippet: description="Password for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service"
findings.recommendation: Aggiungere 'https://github.com/your-repo/.env.example"' nella docstring come reminder

findings.description: Password stored directly in configuration class as default value
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
        description="Password for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
      )
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables for password configuration. Load from .env file at runtime using pydantic_settings with env files

findings.description: User credentials could be exposed if config file is accidentally committed to version control
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_USER: str | None = Field(
        description="Username for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
      )
findings.recommendation: Always this must be derived from environment variables, never default to None in class definition

findings.description: Environment variables should be mandatory for auth fields, not optional with default None
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_HOSTS: str | None = Field(
        description="Hostname or IP address of the Huawei cloud search service instance",
        default=None,
      )
findings.recommendation: Use Field(default=...) to require value at startup, or use field_validator to raise error if missing required auth parameters

findings.description: Missing configurable connection parameters (like DB_TIMEOUT, SSL settings)
findings.code_snippet:
from pydantic import Field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
findings.recommendation: Add additional connection parameters: timeout, connect_timeout, ssl_verify, etc.

findings.description: No validation on minimum/maximum values for host addresses
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_HOSTS: str | None = Field(
        description="Hostname or IP address of the Huawei cloud search service instance",
        default=None,
      )
findings.recommendation: Add field validation using Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=253) for host validation

findings.description: Class docstring uses English despite requesting Italy language in system prompt
findings.code_snippet: Configuration settings for Huawei cloud search service
findings.recommendation: Consider using Italian language in docstrings for Italian project documentation consistency: "Impostazioni di configurazione per il servizio di ricerca Huawei cloud"

findings.description: Credentials (username, password) should not be directly defined in code/config files. Sensitive values should be loaded from environment variables (.env file) to prevent accidental exposure in version control.
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_USER: str | None = Field(
        description="Username for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
    )

    HUAWEI_CLOUD_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
        description="Password for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Move credentials from class fields to environment variables. Set HUAWEI_CLOUD_USER and HUAWEI_CLOUD_PASSWORD as environment variables and load them at runtime using os.environ or pydantic's env file settings.

findings.description: No validation rules or constraints defined for sensitive fields. This could allow invalid or overly sensitive input.
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
        description="Password for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Add validation configurations to ensure passwords meet complexity requirements. Use Field(password=min_length=8) or min_length=8, and add regex patterns for password complexity if needed.

findings.description: Field descriptions may expose sensitive information about what data is being stored. In configurations, avoid including password-related documentation in source code.
findings.code_snippet: description="Password for authenticating with Huawei cloud search service"
findings.recommendation: Remove all credential-related descriptions from the code. Reference external SECURITY.md or configuration docs for credential management guidelines instead.

findings.description: No indication that these values should be sourced from environment variables (settings files). Makes them vulnerable if committed to version control.
findings.code_snippet:
HUAWEI_CLOUD_HOSTS: str | None = Field(
        description="Hostname or IP address of the Huawei cloud search service instance",
        default=None,
    )
findings.recommendation: Configure all cloud credentials and sensitive values via environment variables (.env file) at runtime. Always exclude .env from git with .gitignore.

findings.description: Password is hardcoded in source code instead of using environment variables or .env file
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded password and use environment variable, load from .env file: default=os.getenv("IRIS_PASSWORD", "")

findings.description: Sensitive authentication values (host, user, password, database) should be configured via environment variables, not hardcoded
findings.code_snippet:
IRIS_USER: str | None = Field(
        description="Username for IRIS authentication.",
        default="_SYSTEM",
    )
findings.recommendation: Change defaults to empty string and handle missing env vars gracefully: default=os.getenv("IRIS_USER", "")

findings.description: Default values for connection parameters should be configurable via environment, not source code defaults
findings.recommendation: Use IRIS_SCHEMA = os.getenv("IRIS_SCHEMA", "dify") to load from environment instead of hardcoding defaults

findings.description: Import statement for Field could be simplified since pydantic.models is the module
findings.code_snippet: from pydantic import Field, PositiveInt, model_validator
findings.recommendation: Consider explicit import: from pydantic import Field from Field import PositiveInt, model_validator or use typing import instead

findings.description: Class name convention: BaseSettings already defines the pattern, class name matches standard but could follow app-specific prefix
findings.recommendation: Consider naming convention: IRISVectorConfig or IRISConfig for consistency with app configuration patterns

findings.description: Validation in model_validator uses local variable references instead of runtime os.environ - should use the validated values context
findings.recommendation: Move valid unimportant lines to if __name__ == '__main__' blocker or remove commented code from production

findings.description: Hardcoded infrastructure defaults violate the principle of minimizing hardcoded details. The default value 'api.clickzetta.com' ties the application to a specific endpoint.
findings.code_snippet:
CLICKZETTA_SERVICE: str | None = Field(
    description="Clickzetta API service endpoint (e.g., 'api.clickzetta.com')",
    default="api.clickzetta.com",
)
findings.recommendation: Consider removing the default value entirely or using a generic placeholder. Force the value to be defined in environment variables for better portability and security.

findings.description: Sensitive authentication field 'CLICKZETTA_PASSWORD' allows None as a default. This may lead to connection failures or unexpected behavior at runtime if not enforced.
findings.code_snippet:
CLICKZETTA_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
    description="Password for authenticating with Clickzetta Lakehouse",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Change to 'CLICKZETTA_PASSWORD: str = Field(default=...)' or keep default=None but add a validator to ensure it is not None when 'CLICKZETTA_INSTANCE' is present.

findings.description: Missing type validation for 'CLICKZETTA_ANALYZER_TYPE'. Passing an invalid string will not fail immediately, potentially causing silent failures deep in the application logic.
findings.code_snippet:
CLICKZETTA_ANALYZER_TYPE: str | None = Field(
    description="Analyzer type for full-text search: keyword, english, chinese, unicode",
    default="chinese",
)
findings.recommendation: Use a Set of valid values or an Enum, or add a validator in Pydantic to raise an error for invalid string values.

findings.description: Magic number usage in 'CLICKZETTA_BATCH_SIZE'. The value 100 is defined inline without reference to configuration requirements or tunability.
findings.code_snippet:
CLICKZETTA_BATCH_SIZE: int | None = Field(
    description="Batch size for bulk insert operations",
    default=100,
)
findings.recommendation: Introduce a constant constant like BATCH_SIZE_DEFAULT in the code or util files, and update the Field default to Constant.NAME for clarity.

findings.description: Instance name 'CLICKZETTA_INSTANCE' is optional (str | None). Ensuring database connection correctness requires this to be always present if authentication is used.
findings.code_snippet:
CLICKZETTA_INSTANCE: str | None = Field(
    description="Clickzetta Lakehouse instance ID",
    default=None,
)
findings.recommendation: Add a validator to ensure this field is not None when 'CLICKZETTA_USERNAME' is provided, or set a strict default to make it a required parameter.

findings.description: Variable naming convention uses ALL_CAPS (CLICKZETTA_*). While common for Environment Variable mapping, it is less standard for Python class attributes (PEP 8 suggests snake_case).
findings.recommendation: Consider using snake_case for the class attribute names if the code accesses them directly, and ensure the environment variable mapping is clear. If this class is purely for env loading, ALL_CAPS is acceptable but worth noting for consistency.

findings.description: Password hardcoded as 'Dify@1234' instead of using environment variables or secure vault. This exposes credentials if code is leaked or committed to version control.
findings.code_snippet:
IRIS_PASSWORD: str | None = Field(
    description="Password for IRIS authentication.",
    default="Dify@1234",
)
findings.recommendation: Move password to .env file or use environment variables. Reference: Best Practices - Sensitive values should NEVER be in code, but only in file .env

findings.description: Username '_SYSTEM' hardcoded, which is an administrative account. This could expose administrative access.
findings.code_snippet:
IRIS_USER: str | None = Field(
    description="Username for IRIS authentication.",
    default="_SYSTEM",
)
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or load username from .env file

findings.description: IRIS_HOST defaults to localhost with no validation. If this runs in a multi-tenant environment, it could expose connection to internal services.
findings.code_snippet:
IRIS_HOST: str | None = Field(
    description="Hostname or IP address of the IRIS server.",
    default="localhost",
)
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitize IRIS_HOST. Require HTTPS for external connections. Implement connection validation
